350	\section{Representability of the Quot scheme}
351	\label{sec:quot_representable}
352	
353	\begin{theorem}\leanok
354	[Representability of the Quot functor]
355	  \label{thm:quot_representable}
356	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme}
357	  
358	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Main Existence Theorems"
359	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
360	  % L2220-L2273); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.
361	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
362	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi: X\to S\) a projective morphism,
363	  %  and \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\). Then for any
364	  %  coherent \({\mathcal O}_X\)-module \(E\) and any polynomial \(\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]\),
365	  %  the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) is representable
366	  %  by a projective \(S\)-scheme \(\quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\)."
367	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, Theorem (Grothendieck), Theorem
368	  (Altman--Kleiman) (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.}
369	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a projective morphism,
370	  \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent
371	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Then the
372	  functor \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) of \cref{def:quot_functor} is
373	  representable by a projective \(S\)-scheme \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\),
374	  equipped with a universal quotient
375	  \[
376	    q^{\mathrm{univ}} :
377	    \pi^*_{\Quot} E \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}^{\mathrm{univ}}
378	    \quad \text{on } X \times_S \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S},
379	  \]
380	  flat over \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) with constant Hilbert polynomial
381	  \(\Phi\) on every fiber. The Hilbert scheme is the special case
382	  \(E = \mathcal{O}_X\): \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} = \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\)
383	  represents the functor of \(T\)-flat closed subschemes
384	  \(Y \subset X_T\) with fiberwise Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\).
385	\end{theorem}
386	
387	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: this proof is long (~5 named sub-sections in [Nitsure]
388	% \S 5). Each sub-step is recorded with its own SOURCE QUOTE in the
389	% sub-lemmas below. The structural skeleton of the proof is restated in
390	% project notation in the \begin{proof} block.
391	\begin{proof}
392	  
393	  The proof has four steps; each step is a sub-lemma in the next
394	  sub-sections.
395	  \begin{enumerate}
396	    \item \textbf{(Boundedness, \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}.)} Pick the
397	      Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound $m = m(\mathrm{rank}\,V,
398	      \mathrm{rank}\,W, \Phi)$ uniformly across all fibers (by Mumford's
399	      \(m\)-regularity theorem applied to \(E_s\) and to all coherent
400	      quotients of \(E_s\) with Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\)). For \(r \ge m\)
401	      and any \(T \to S\) and any \(T\)-flat quotient
402	      \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) with Hilbert polynomial
403	      \(\Phi\), the higher direct images \(R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)\)
404	      and \(R^i (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)\) and \(R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)\)
405	      vanish for \(i \ge 1\) (where \(\mathcal{G} = \ker q\)), and the
406	      direct images \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)\), \((\pi_T)_* E_T(r)\),
407	      \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)\) are locally free of ranks fixed by the
408	      data; the canonical maps \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)\)
409	      are surjective. Cohomology-and-base-change (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology},
410	      Stacks tag 02KH) propagates these properties across \(T\)-base change.
411	    \item \textbf{(Grassmannian embedding, \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}.)}
412	      Reduce by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} to the case
413	      \(X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\) with \(V, W\) locally free
414	      on \(S\), \(L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)\). Fix \(r \ge m\).
415	      The Grassmannian embedding
416	      \[
417	        \alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
418	        \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
419	          W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
420	        \right)
421	      \]
422	      sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\) to the locally free
423	      quotient \((\pi_T)_*(q(r)) : (\pi_T)_* E_T(r) \to (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)\).
424	      This is injective on \(T\)-valued points: from the map $T \to
425	      \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$ one recovers
426	      the kernel-pull-back \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r) \to E_T(r)\),
427	      whose cokernel is \(q(r) : E_T(r) \to \mathcal{F}(r)\);
428	      twisting back by \(-r\) recovers \(q\).
429	    \item \textbf{(Locally closed in the Grassmannian via flattening
430	      stratification.)} Let $T_0 = \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V,
431	      \Phi(r))$ with universal quotient
432	      \(f : (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_{T_0} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{J}\)
433	      and kernel \(\mathcal{K}\). Form the composite
434	      $h : \pi_{T_0}^* \mathcal{K} \hookrightarrow
435	      \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V) = \pi_{T_0}^* (\pi_{T_0})_* E_{T_0}
436	      \twoheadrightarrow E_{T_0}$
437	      and let \(q := \mathrm{coker}(h) : E_{T_0} \to \mathcal{F}\). By the
438	      flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
439	      of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\)
440	      on \(X_{T_0}\), there exists a locally closed subscheme
441	      \(T_0^\Phi \subseteq T_0\) such that for any \(\phi : Y \to T_0\), the
442	      pulled-back cokernel \(\mathcal{F}_Y\) is \(Y\)-flat with fiberwise
443	      Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\) iff \(\phi\) factors through \(T_0^\Phi\).
444	      The pair \((T_0^\Phi, \text{universal cokernel})\) represents
445	      \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\).
446	    \item \textbf{(Closed, hence projective,
447	      \cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}.)} The functor
448	      \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) satisfies the valuative criterion of
449	      properness with respect to DVRs (Exercise (7) of \S 1, proved
450	      using that flatness over a DVR is torsion-freeness). Combined
451	      with the locally closed embedding of step 3 into the projective
452	      $\mathrm{Gr}_S \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^{\Phi(r)}
453	      (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V))$, this upgrades to a \emph{closed}
454	      embedding. Hence \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is a projective \(S\)-scheme.
455	  \end{enumerate}
456	  Finally, by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}, the general case
457	  (arbitrary \(X\) projective over \(S\) and arbitrary coherent \(E\)) follows
458	  from the case \(X = \mathbb{P}(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\): pick a closed
459	  embedding \(X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)\) and write \(E\) as a coherent quotient
460	  of \(\pi^*(W)(\nu)\) for some vector bundle \(W\) on \(S\) and integer \(\nu\);
461	  the Quot scheme of the original data is then a closed subscheme of the
462	  Quot scheme of the universal data, by the closed-embedding clause of
463	  \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}.
464	\end{proof}
465	
466	\section{Sub-lemmas of the construction}
467	\label{sec:quot_sub_lemmas}
468	
469	\begin{lemma}[Reduction to the universal case]
470	  \label{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}
471	  
472	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Reduction to the case of
473	  % \(\Quot_{\pi^*W/\PP(V)/S}^{\Phi,L}\)"
474	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
475	  % L2294-L2332).
476	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
477	  % "It is enough to prove Theorem REF in the special case
478	  %  that \(X = \PP(V)\) and
479	  %  \(E= \pi^*(W)\) where \(V\) and \(W\) are vector bundles on \(S\), as
480	  %  a consequence of the next lemma.
481	  %
482	  %  \begin{lemma}
483	  %  (i) Let \(\nu\) be any integer.
484	  %  Then tensoring by \(L^{\nu}\) gives an isomorphism of
485	  %  functors from \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) to
486	  %  \(\Quot_{E(\nu)/X/S}^{\Psi,L}\) where the polynomial
487	  %  \(\Psi \in \Q[\lambda]\) is defined by \(\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)\).
488	  %
489	  %  (ii) Let \(\phi : E \to G\) be a surjective homomorphism of
490	  %  coherent sheaves on \(X\). Then the corresponding natural transformation
491	  %  \(\Quot_{G/X/S}^{\Phi,L} \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\)
492	  %  is a closed embedding.
493	  %  \end{lemma}"
494	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
495	  Let \(S\), \(X\), \(L\), \(E\), \(\Phi\) be as in \cref{def:quot_functor}.
496	  \begin{enumerate}
497	    \item (Twist-shift.) For \(\nu \in \mathbb{Z}\), tensoring by
498	      \(L^{\otimes \nu}\) gives an isomorphism of functors
499	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \xrightarrow{\sim}
500	      \Quot^{\Psi,L}_{E(\nu)/X/S}\( with \)\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
501	    \item (Surjection induces closed embedding.) Given a surjection
502	      \(\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G\) of coherent sheaves on \(X\), the
503	      induced natural transformation
504	      \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \to \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) is a closed
505	      embedding of functors.
506	  \end{enumerate}
507	  Consequently, if \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\pi^* W / \mathbb{P}(V)/S}\) is
508	  representable for all \(V\), \(W\) vector bundles on \(S\), then so is
509	  \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) for every \(X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)\) closed and
510	  every coherent \(E\) on \(X\) that is a quotient of \(\pi^*(W)(\nu)|_X\) for
511	  some \(W\) and \(\nu\).
512	\end{lemma}
513	
514	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The statement (i) is obvious. The statement (ii) just
515	% says that given any locally noetherian scheme \(T\) and a family
516	% \(\langle \F,q\rangle \in \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(T)\), there exists
517	% a closed subscheme \(T' \subset T\) with the following universal property:
518	% for any locally noetherian scheme \(U\) and a morphism \(f: U\to T\),
519	% the pulled back homomorphism of \({\mathcal O}_{X_U}\)-modules \(q_U : E_U \to \F_U\)
520	% factors via the pulled back homomorphism \(\phi_U : E_U \to G_U\)
521	% if and only if \(U \to T\) factors via \(T'\hra T\).
522	% This is satisfied by taking \(T'\) to be the vanishing scheme
523	% for the composite homomorphism \(\ker(\phi) \hra  E \stackrel{q}{\to} \F\)
524	% of coherent sheaves on \(X_T\) (see Remark REF),
525	% which makes sense here as both \(\ker(\phi)\) and \(\F\) are coherent
526	% on \(X_T\) and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\)."
527	\begin{proof}
528	  
529	  (i) is direct from the definitions: tensoring with \(L^{\otimes \nu}\) is
530	  an autoequivalence of coherent sheaves on \(X\) (and on every \(X_T\)),
531	  preserving surjectivity, \(T\)-flatness, and properness of support, and
532	  shifting the Hilbert polynomial by \(\nu\) in the variable.
533	
534	  (ii) Given a family \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\) in
535	  \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\), the locus where \(q\) factors through
536	  \(\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G\) is cut out by the vanishing of the
537	  composite $\ker(\phi)|_{X_T} \hookrightarrow E_T \xrightarrow{q}
538	  \mathcal{F}\(. Because \)\mathcal{F}\( is \)T$-flat, the schematic image of
539	  this composite descends to a closed subscheme \(T' \subset T\) such that
540	  \(f : U \to T\) factors through \(T'\) iff \(q_U\) factors through \(\phi_U\).
541	  Hence \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \hookrightarrow \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\)
542	  is a closed embedding of subfunctors.
543	\end{proof}
544	
545	\begin{lemma}[Uniform Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound]
546	  \label{lem:quot_boundedness}
547	  
548	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"
549	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
550	  % L2346-L2404). Builds on [Nitsure] \S 2 (Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity)
551	  % and \S 3 (semi-continuity and base-change), cited internally as
552	  % Theorem~"Mumford" and Theorem~"Base-change for flat sheaves".
553	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
554	  % "We consider the sheaf \(E = \pi^*(W)\) on \(X=\PP(V)\) where
555	  %  \(V\) is a vector bundle on \(S\),
556	  %  and take \(L = {\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)}(1)\).
557	  %  For any field \(k\) and a \(k\)-valued point
558	  %  \(s\) of \(S\), we have an isomorphism
559	  %  \(\PP(V)_s \simeq \PP^n_k\) where \(n=\rank(V)-1\),
560	  %  and the restricted sheaf \(E_s\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) is isomorphic to
561	  %  \(\oplus^p{\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)_s}\) where \(p=\rank(W)\).
562	  %  It follows from Theorem REF
563	  %  that given any \(\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]\), there exists an integer
564	  %  \(m\) which depends only on \(\rank(V)\), \(\rank(W)\) and \(\Phi\),
565	  %  such that for any field \(k\) and a \(k\)-valued point \(s\) of \(S\),
566	  %  the sheaf \(E_s\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) is \(m\)-regular, and for any
567	  %  coherent quotient \(q: E_s \to \F\) on \(\PP(V)_s\) with
568	  %  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\), the sheaf \(\F\) and the kernel sheaf
569	  %  \(\G\subset E_s\) of \(q\) are both \(m\)-regular."
570	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity" (FGA Explained
571	  Ch.~5).}
572	  Let \(X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\) with \(V\), \(W\) vector bundles on
573	  \(S\) of ranks \(n+1\), \(p\), \(L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)\), and
574	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). There exists an integer
575	  \(m = m(n, p, \Phi)\), depending only on \(n\), \(p\), and \(\Phi\), such that
576	  for every geometric point \(s\) of \(S\) and every coherent quotient
577	  \(q : E_s \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) with Hilbert polynomial
578	  \(\Phi\), the sheaves \(E_s\), \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(\ker q\) on \(X_s\) are all
579	  \(m\)-regular in the sense of Castelnuovo--Mumford. Consequently, for
580	  every \(T \to S\) and every \(T\)-flat quotient
581	  \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) on \(X_T\) with Hilbert
582	  polynomial \(\Phi\) and every \(r \ge m\), the conclusions \textbf{(*)} and
583	  \textbf{(**)} of \cite[\S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"]{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
584	  hold: the direct images \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)\), \((\pi_T)_* E_T(r)\),
585	  \((\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)\) are locally free of fixed ranks; the
586	  canonical surjections \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \twoheadrightarrow (-)(r)\)
587	  hold; and the higher direct images \(R^i (\pi_T)_* (-)(r)\) vanish for
588	  \(i \ge 1\).
589	\end{lemma}
590	
591	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of \(m\)-Regularity"
592	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2346-L2404).
593	% The proof invokes the m-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2
594	% (Theorem "Mumford") to extract the uniform bound m, and the
595	% base-change-for-flat-sheaves theorem of [Nitsure] \S 3 (cited as
596	% "Base-change for flat sheaves") to propagate the conclusions across \(T\).
597	\begin{proof}
598	  
599	  Each geometric fiber \(E_s\) is a trivial bundle on \(\mathbb{P}^n_{\kappa(s)}\)
600	  of rank \(p\), so \(E_s\) is \(0\)-regular. The Castelnuovo--Mumford
601	  \(m\)-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2 produces, for any fixed Hilbert
602	  polynomial \(\Phi\), a uniform integer \(m = m(n, p, \Phi)\) above which
603	  every coherent quotient of \(E_s\) with Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi\) -- and
604	  its kernel -- is \(m\)-regular. The Castelnuovo Lemma of [Nitsure] \S 2
605	  converts \(m\)-regularity into vanishing of \(H^i((-) (r))\) for \(i \ge 1\)
606	  and \(r \ge m\), and into global generation of \(H^0((-)(r))\). To
607	  propagate the fiber-wise statement to families over \(T\), apply the
608	  flat-base-change theorem (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}, Stacks
609	  tag 02KH) along \(T \to S\): cohomology of flat families commutes with
610	  base change, so the direct images \((\pi_T)_*(-)(r)\) are locally free of
611	  the rank dictated by the fiber Hilbert polynomials, and the canonical
612	  maps \((\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)\) inherit fiberwise
613	  surjectivity.
614	\end{proof}
615	
616	\begin{lemma}[Grassmannian embedding \(\alpha\) is injective]
617	  \label{lem:quot_alpha_injective}
618	  
619	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
620	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
621	  % L2409-L2451).
622	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
623	  % "We now fix a positive integer \(r\) such that \(r\ge m\).
624	  %  Note that the rank of \({\pi_T}_*\F(r)\) is \(\Phi(r)\)
625	  %  and \(\pi_*E(r) = W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V\).
626	  %  Therefore the surjective homomorphism
627	  %  \({\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\)
628	  %  defines an element of the set
629	  %  \(\Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V , \Phi(r))(T)\).
630	  %  We thus get a morphism of functors
631	  %  \(\)\alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \to
632	  %  \Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V, \Phi(r))\(\)
633	  %  It associates to \(q: E_T \to \F\) the quotient
634	  %  \({\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\).
635	  %
636	  %  The above morphism \(\alpha\) is injective because the quotient
637	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) can be recovered from
638	  %  \({\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)\)
639	  %  as follows."
640	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
641	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
642	  In the universal setup \(X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)\), \(E = \pi^* W\), fix
643	  \(r \ge m\) from \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}. The natural transformation
644	  \[
645	    \alpha :
646	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
647	    \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
648	      W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
649	    \right),
650	    \qquad
651	    \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle \longmapsto
652	    \langle (\pi_T)_*\mathcal{F}(r),\, (\pi_T)_*(q(r))\rangle
653	  \]
654	  is well-defined (the target is locally free of rank \(\Phi(r)\), and the
655	  canonical isomorphism $\pi_* E(r) \cong W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S}
656	  \mathrm{Sym}^r V\( gives the source) and \emph{injective} on \)T$-points:
657	  the data of the locally free quotient at level \(r\) determines
658	  \(q : E_T \to \mathcal{F}\).
659	\end{lemma}
660	
661	\begin{proof}
662	  
663	  Suppose \(\alpha(T)\) sends both \(\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle\) and
664	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle\) to the same \(T\)-point of the
665	  Grassmannian. Then the pulled-back kernel
666	  $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \to (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)
667	  = \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_T$
668	  is the same. The diagram \textbf{(**)} of
669	  \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} gives a right-exact sequence
670	  \[
671	    (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \xrightarrow{h}
672	    E_T(r) \xrightarrow{q_i(r)} \mathcal{F}_i(r) \to 0,
673	  \]
674	  so \(q_i(r)\) is recovered as the cokernel of \(h\); twisting by \(-r\)
675	  recovers \(q_i\), giving \(q_1 = q_2\) and hence
676	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle\).
677	\end{proof}
678	
679	\begin{lemma}[Valuative criterion of properness for \(\Quot\)]
680	  \label{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
681	  
682	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
683	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
684	  % L2510-L2542). Original reference: EGA IV(2) 2.8.1, cited there.
685	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
686	  % "The functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) satisfies the following valuative
687	  %  criterion for properness over \(S\): given any discrete valuation ring
688	  %  \(R\) over \(S\) with quotient field \(K\), the restriction map
689	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R) \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\(\)
690	  %  is bijective. This can be seen as follows.
691	  %  Given any coherent quotient
692	  %  \(q: E_K \to \F\) on \(X_R\) which defines an element \(\langle \F, q\rangle\)
693	  %  of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\).
694	  %  Let \(\ov{\F}\) be the image of the composite homomorphism
695	  %  \(E_R \to j_*(E_K) \to j_*\F\) where \(j : X_K \hra X_R\) is the open
696	  %  inclusion. Let \(\ov{q} : E_R\to \ov{\F}\) be the induced surjection.
697	  %  Then we leave it to the reader to verify that
698	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) is an element of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R)\)
699	  %  which maps to \(\langle \F, q\rangle\), and is the unique such element.
700	  %  (Use the basic fact that being flat over a dvr
701	  %  is the same as being torsion-free.)"
702	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
703	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5); EGA IV(2) 2.8.1.}
704	  The functor \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) satisfies the valuative criterion
705	  for properness over \(S\): for every discrete valuation ring \(R\) with
706	  fraction field \(K\) and every morphism \(\Spec R \to S\), the restriction
707	  map
708	  \[
709	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec R)
710	    \longrightarrow
711	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec K)
712	  \]
713	  is bijective.
714	\end{lemma}
715	
716	\begin{proof}
717	  
718	  Given a \(\Spec K\)-point \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) on \(X_K\),
719	  let \(j : X_K \hookrightarrow X_R\) be the open inclusion. Define
720	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) as the image of the composite
721	  \(E_R \to j_* E_K \to j_* \mathcal{F}_K\), with induced surjection
722	  \(\overline{q} : E_R \to \overline{\mathcal{F}}\). The sheaf
723	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) is torsion-free over \(R\) (being a subsheaf of
724	  \(j_* \mathcal{F}_K\)), hence \(R\)-flat (flatness over a DVR
725	  \(=\) torsion-freeness), and restricts on the generic fiber to
726	  \(\mathcal{F}_K\). The pair \((\overline{\mathcal{F}}, \overline{q})\) is
727	  the unique extension of \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) to a
728	  \(\Spec R\)-point (uniqueness: any other extension agrees on the dense
729	  open \(X_K \subset X_R\) and is torsion-free, hence equals the image
730	  construction). The Hilbert polynomial is constant on connected
731	  \(\Spec R\) by flatness.
732	\end{proof}
733	
734	\section{Cohomology and base change}
735	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
736	
737	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
738	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
739	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
740	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
741	
742	\begin{theorem}\leanok
743	[Flat base change of cohomology]
744	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
745	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
746	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)
747	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947-L970).
748	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
749	  % "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
750	  %  \(\)
751	  %  \xymatrix{
752	  %  X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
753	  %  S' \ar[r]^g & S
754	  %  }
755	  %  \(\)
756	  %  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
757	  %  with pullback \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\).
758	  %  Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\) is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
759	  %  For any \(i \geq 0\)
760	  %  \begin{enumerate}
761	  %  \item the base change map of
762	  %  Cohomology, Lemma REF
763	  %  is an isomorphism
764	  %  \(\)
765	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
766	  %  \(\)
767	  %  \item if \(S = \Spec(A)\) and \(S' = \Spec(B)\), then
768	  %  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\).
769	  %  \end{enumerate}"
770	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
771	  Let
772	  \[
773	    \begin{array}{ccc}
774	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
775	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
776	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
777	    \end{array}
778	  \]
779	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with \(g\) flat and \(f\) quasi-compact and
780	  quasi-separated. Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
781	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module with pull-back
782	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}\). For every \(i \ge 0\):
783	  \begin{enumerate}
784	    \item the base-change map
785	      \(g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'\) is an isomorphism;
786	    \item if \(S = \Spec A\) and \(S' = \Spec B\), then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
787	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
788	  \end{enumerate}
789	\end{theorem}
790	
791	\section{Lean encoding}
792	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
793	
794	The Lean target file is the new
795	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
796	proceeds in three phases.
797	
798	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
799	On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
800	coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
801	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
802	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
803	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
804	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
805	property of \(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
806	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
807	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
808	
809	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
810	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
811	\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is encoded as the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
812	affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
813	\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
814	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
815	universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
816	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
817	\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
818	\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement uses
819	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
820	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
821	\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\).
822	
823	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
824	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
825	Grassmannian
826	\(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\), via:
827	\begin{enumerate}
828	  \item the uniform \(m\)-regularity bound from
829	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
830	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
831	  \item the Grassmannian embedding \(\alpha\) from
832	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
833	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
834	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
835	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
836	    stratum \(T_0^\Phi\);
837	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
838	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
839	    projective \(S\)-scheme.
840	\end{enumerate}
841	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
842	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
843	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
844	
845	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
846	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
847	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
848	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
849	full \(R^i f_*\) statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
850	pinned commit.
851	
852	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
853	This file directly consumes
854	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
855	gluing infrastructure and
856	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
857	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
858	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
859	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
860	\(E = \mathcal{O}_C\) on a smooth proper curve \(C/k\), giving the Hilbert
861	scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
862	and its open subscheme \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) of relative effective divisors.
863	
864	\section{Out of scope}
865	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
866	
867	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
868	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
869	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
870	\begin{itemize}
871	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
872	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
873	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
874	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
875	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
876	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot scheme and is
877	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
878	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford \(m\)-regularity theorem} -- input to
879	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
880	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
881	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
882	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
883	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
884	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
885	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
886	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
887	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
888	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
889	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
890	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
891	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
892	  \item \textbf{Hironaka's counterexample to representability of
893	    \(\Hilb_{X/\mathbb{C}}\) in the non-projective case} (Nitsure \S 1,
894	    Exercise (8)) -- pedagogical only; Route A operates entirely in the
895	    projective setting.
896	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
897	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream of the Quot existence
898	    statement; its blueprint home is
899	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
900	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
901	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
902	    constructed in this chapter.
903	\end{itemize}

1	# Analogy: affine-open section formula for `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	quotscheme-pullback-affine-section
8	
9	## Iteration
10	182
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	For a morphism `g : S' ⟶ S` of schemes, `N : S.Modules`, and an
15	affine open `U : S'.Opens`, is there a Mathlib lemma identifying
16	`Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U)` with
17	`Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, V)} Γ(N, V)` for any compatible affine `V ⊆ S`?
18	
19	## Project artifact(s)
20	
21	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:430–434` —
22	  `pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section` (rfl, axiom-clean
23	  iter-181; closes the *trivial* RHS bridge).
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:468–499` —
25	  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`,
26	  body `:= sorry`. ~30 LOC of docstring spelling the substantive Mathlib
27	  gap; transitively taints downstream
28	  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso` and `flatBaseChangeCohomology`.
29	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:527–549` —
30	  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` (general-base
31	  helper) waits on the base-side Mayer-Vietoris (gap (b) below).
32	- `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex:740–795` —
33	  `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH); explicitly identified
34	  in `Picard_QuotScheme.tex:851–856` as a project-side bridge ("Mathlib
35	  carries a partial form … the full `R^i f_*` statement is a project-side
36	  bridge if not present at the pinned commit").
37	
38	## Mathlib precedents examined
39	
40	| Mathlib symbol | Location | What it gives | Why it doesn't close the gap |
41	|---|---|---|---|
42	| `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback` | `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:167` | `Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules`, defined as `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` | abstract; no closed-form `pullback_obj_obj` simp lemma analogous to `pushforward_obj_obj` |
43	| `SheafOfModules.pullback` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean:53` | `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` | constructed via `partial adjoint`; sections are not exposed |
44	| `SheafOfModules.pullbackIso` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean:105` | `pullback φ ≅ forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback ⋙ sheafification` | factors through sheafification, which destroys per-affine-open identification |
45	| `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean:117` | `sheafification ⋙ pullback ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback ⋙ sheafification` | tells us "pullback commutes with sheafification" — still does not give the affine-open section formula |
46	| `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackFree.lean:122` | `(pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I` | **only for FREE sheaves** — too restrictive for arbitrary `N : S.Modules` |
47	| `PresheafOfModules.pullback` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44` | `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` | also abstract; sections still not exposed (only `pushforward_obj_obj` has a closed form, not `pullback_obj_obj`) |
48	| `AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackSpecIso` | `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Pullbacks.lean` | scheme-level iso `Spec S ×_{Spec R} Spec T ≅ Spec (S ⊗_R T)` | base-scheme pullback, NOT module-pullback. Provides ring data for the target but does not identify `(g')*F` at sections |
49	| `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` | `Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/Stability.lean:90` | `IsBaseChange S f → Flat S N` | the *algebraic atom* — confirms preservation of flatness once the iso is in hand, but does not produce the iso itself |
50	| `IsBaseChange.equiv` | `Mathlib/RingTheory/IsTensorProduct.lean:375` | `S ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[S] N` from `IsBaseChange S f` | the actual linear iso; this is what one would *consume* once the LHS bridge yields an `IsBaseChange` witness |
51	| `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.flat_appLE` | `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Flat.lean` | flatness of `Scheme.Hom.appLE g U V _ : Γ(S, V) →+* Γ(S', U)` for affine opens | the ring-level flatness hypothesis the affine-base helper would feed `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` |
52	| `Module.Flat.casesOn` — same file | per-affine flat ring map hypothesis | matches `Flat.mk` pattern of `[Flat g]` | extracts the hypothesis, not the iso |
53

57	*no* lemma producing the section identification. The only closed-form

66	## Decisions identified
67	
68	### Decision 1: Affine-open section formula for `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
69	
70	- **Mathlib idiom**: There is **none**. `Scheme.Modules.pullback g` is
71	  abstract (`pushforward g`'s `leftAdjoint`), built through partial-adjoint
72	  machinery. The only closed-form section lemma is `pullbackObjFreeIso`
73	  *at free sheaves*. Cited:
74	  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:167`,
75	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean:53`,
76	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackFree.lean:122`. The
77	  blueprint explicitly identifies this as a project-side bridge
78	  (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:851–856`).
79	- **Project's current path**:
80	  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`
81	  carries a typed `sorry` waiting for the bridge. The intended use is
82	  `IsBaseChange.equiv` applied to a witness produced from the ring map
83	  `Scheme.Hom.appLE g ⊤ U _ : Γ(S, ⊤) →+* Γ(S', U)` (flat by
84	  `Flat.flat_appLE`).
85	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (in the sense that **no Mathlib idiom
86	  exists**, so consuming it as a `sorry` is the only option until the
87	  bridge is built).
88	- **Cost of divergence**: TWO bodies sit on this gap (`_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`
89	  AND, transitively, the general-base `_of_isAffineOpen`, plus
90	  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`, `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`,
91	  and `flatBaseChangeCohomology`). 4 helpers, 2 iters of lane work, no
92	  payoff. Each iter another helper appears around the gap, not closing it.
93	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL → BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER.** The
94	  helper required is a new project-side definition:
95	  ```lean
96	  /-- Sections of the pullback of `Scheme.Modules` over a compatible
97	  affine pair `(V ⊆ S, U ⊆ S')` identify with the tensor product. -/
98	  noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pullback_app_isoTensor
99	      {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (N : X.Modules)
100	      {U : Y.Opens} {V : X.Opens}
101	      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
102	      (e : U ≤ (Opens.map g.base).obj V) :
103	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
104	        TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) Γ(N, V) := sorry
105	  ```
106	  (or the analogous `IsBaseChange` statement). The fact that **the
107	  presheaf-level pullback is also abstract** (`PresheafOfModules.pullback`
108	  is also a partial-adjoint construction with no `pullback_obj_obj`
109	  closed form, cf.
110	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`) means
111	  one cannot collapse the proof through the presheaf level either —
112	  the body has to be built directly. The standard Mathlib trick for
113	  affine targets is the **Tilde** route
114	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean`): on `Spec R`,
115	  `tilde M` is the QC sheaf with `Γ(tilde M, ⊤) = M` (`tilde.isoTop`),
116	  and `(pullback g).obj (tilde N) ≅ tilde (Γ(S', U) ⊗_R N)` would be the
117	  corresponding identification on Spec rings. Promoting this from the
118	  Spec/tilde setting to a general affine open in `S'` is essentially
119	  Stacks tag 01I8 / 01HQ machinery — also absent at the pinned commit.
120	
121	### Decision 2: `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` consumer-side packaging
122	
123	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsBaseChange.equiv : S ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[S] N`
124	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/IsTensorProduct.lean:375`) **is** the right
125	  consumer. Once an `IsBaseChange (algebraMap R S) (η.toLinearMap)`
126	  witness is in hand for the affine-open ring data, this single
127	  declaration produces the iso. `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`
128	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/Stability.lean:90`) is sound for the
129	  flatness *consequence* but is not the iso witness.
130	- **Project's current path**: The blueprint and the lemma docstring
131	  already plan to use `IsBaseChange.equiv`. Good fit.
132	- **Gap**: identical.
133	- **Verdict**: PROCEED. Once Decision 1's helper lands, this is a
134	  3–5 LOC consumption (compose `pullback_app_isoTensor` with
135	  `IsBaseChange.equiv.symm`, then conclude `IsIso` via
136	  `LinearEquiv.toEquiv.bijective` and reflective comparison with the
137	  Beck-Chevalley arrow).
138	
139	### Decision 3: Base-side Mayer-Vietoris reduction (general `S`)
140	
141	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib has `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
142	  (`Sheaf.lean:131-135`) which reduces "iso of `S'.Modules` morphism" to
143	  "iso on every `U : S'.Opens`". It does NOT reduce "iso on every open"
144	  to "iso on a basis" for `Scheme.Modules`. For sheaves on topological
145	  spaces, "iso on stalks ⟹ iso" (`TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor` +
146	  `ReflectsIsomorphisms`) is the canonical route, but our morphism is
147	  in `Scheme.Modules`, not stalks. There is currently no
148	  `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app_isAffineOpen` lemma.
149	- **Project's current path**: Body of `_of_isAffineOpen` is a typed
150	  `sorry` planning a refinement of affine `U` along affine cover of `S`,
151	  then descent via `QuasiSeparated f`. The project already has the
152	  *target-side* dual (`_of_affineCover` — also a sorry) which is the
153	  exact symmetric problem.
154	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (no idiom exists; symmetric to gap (b)).
155	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER.** Two routes:
156	  1. Build a project-side `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app_isAffineOpen`
157	     (general-purpose), then apply it on both target and base sides. This
158	     subsumes the existing `_of_affineCover` helper as well — best ROI.
159	  2. Direct route: build base-side `_of_baseAffineCover` paralleling
160	     the existing `_of_affineCover`, both still as project-side helpers.
161

Body needs to either: (a) trace through `pullbackIso` + sheafification = identity on affine opens (because affine opens are sheafification-stable; reduces to the presheaf-pullback identification), OR (b) use the universal property of `pullback` via `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and exhibit the tensor product as the universal `Γ(Y, U)`-module receiving a `Γ(X, V)`-linear map from `Γ(N, V)` |

16	/-!
17	
18	# Construction of M^~
19	
20	Given any commutative ring `R` and `R`-module `M`, we construct the sheaf `M^~` of `𝒪_SpecR`-modules
21	such that `M^~(U)` is the set of dependent functions that are locally fractions.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	* `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` : `M^~` as a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules.
25	* `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction` : `~` is left adjoint to taking global sections.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public noncomputable section
30	
31	universe u
32	
33	open TopCat AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory Opposite
34	
35	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	open _root_.PrimeSpectrum
40	
41	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules. -/
42	def modulesSpecToSheaf :
43	    (Spec R).Modules ⥤ TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat R) (Spec R) :=
44	  SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (.op ⊤)
45	    (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop) ⋙
46	  sheafCompose _ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R).hom.hom)
47	
48	/-- The global section functor for `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules -/
49	noncomputable
50	def moduleSpecΓFunctor : (Spec (.of R)).Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R :=
51	  modulesSpecToSheaf ⋙ TopCat.Sheaf.forget _ _ ⋙ (evaluation _ _).obj (.op ⊤)
52	
53	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
54	open PrimeSpectrum in
55	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules is fully faithful. -/
56	def SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful : (modulesSpecToSheaf (R := R)).FullyFaithful where
57	  preimage {M N} f := ⟨fun U ↦ ModuleCat.ofHom ⟨(f.1.app U).hom.toAddHom, by
58	    intro t m
59	    apply TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).2
60	    intro x hxU
61	    obtain ⟨a, ⟨_, ⟨r, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxr, hrU : basicOpen _ ≤ _⟩ :=
62	      PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU U.unop.2
63	    refine ⟨_, hrU, hxr, ?_⟩
64	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (N.val.map_smul (homOfLE hrU).op t _).symm
65	    change N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _) = _ • N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _)
66	    have (x : _) :
67	        f.1.app _ (M.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op _) = N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ x) :=
68	      congr($(f.1.naturality (homOfLE hrU).op).hom x)
69	    rw [← this, ← this, M.val.map_smul]
70	    generalize (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE hrU).op t = t
71	    letI := Module.compHom (R := Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r)) Γ(M, basicOpen r)
72	      (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
73	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) :=
74	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
75	    letI := Module.compHom Γ(N, basicOpen r) (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
76	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r) :=
77	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
78	    exact (IsLocalization.linearMap_compatibleSMul (.powers (M := R) r)
79	      Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r)).map_smul
80	      (f.hom.app _).hom _ _⟩, fun i ↦ by ext x; exact congr($(f.1.naturality i).hom x)⟩
81	  map_preimage f := rfl
82	  preimage_map f := rfl
83	
84	/--
85	`M^~` as a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules
86	-/
87	def tilde : (Spec R).Modules where
88	  val := moduleStructurePresheaf R M
89	  isSheaf := (TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp (forget AddCommGrpCat) _).2
90	    (structureSheafInType R M).2
91	
92	namespace tilde
93	
94	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
95	/-- (Implementation). The image of `tilde` under `modulesSpecToSheaf` is isomorphic to
96	`structurePresheafInModuleCat`. They are defeq as types but the `Smul` instance are not defeq. -/
97	noncomputable
98	def modulesSpecToSheafIso :
99	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).1 ≅ structurePresheafInModuleCat R M :=
100	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun U ↦ LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
101	    (X₁ := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj _)
102	    { __ := AddEquiv.refl _,
103	      map_smul' r m := IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (M := ((structureSheafInType R M).obj.obj U))
104	        ((structureSheafInType R R).obj.obj U) r m }) fun _ ↦ rfl
105	
106	/-- The map from `M` to `Γ(M, U)`. This is a localization map when `U = D(f)`. -/
107	def toOpen (U : (Spec R).Opens) : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj (.op U) :=
108	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M U) ≫ ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).inv
109	
110	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
111	theorem toOpen_res (U V : Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R)) (i : V ⟶ U) :
112	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op = toOpen M V :=
113	  rfl
114	
115	instance (f : R) : IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom :=
116	  .of_linearEquiv (.powers f) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M (basicOpen f))
117	    ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).toLinearEquiv.symm
118	
119	noncomputable
120	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) : Module R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
121	  inferInstanceAs (Module R ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf x))
122	
123	/--
124	If `x` is a point of `Spec R`, this is the morphism of `R`-modules from `M` to the stalk of
125	`M^~` at `x`.
126	-/
127	noncomputable def toStalk (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
128	    ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ ModuleCat.of R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
129	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..)
130	
131	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
132	    IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (toStalk M x).hom :=
133	  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..))
134	
135	/-- The tilde construction is functorial. -/
136	protected noncomputable def map {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) : tilde M ⟶ tilde N :=
137	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage ⟨(modulesSpecToSheafIso M).hom ≫
138	    { app U := ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.comapₗ f.hom _ _ .rfl) } ≫
139	    (modulesSpecToSheafIso N).inv⟩
140	
141	@[simp, reassoc]
142	protected lemma map_id {M : ModuleCat R} : tilde.map (𝟙 M) = 𝟙 _ := by
143	  ext p x
144	  exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (LocalizedModule.map_id _) _)
145	
146	@[simp, reassoc]
147	protected lemma map_comp {M N P : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :
148	    tilde.map (f ≫ g) = tilde.map f ≫ tilde.map g := by
149	  ext p x
150	  exact Subtype.ext (funext
151	    fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.map_comp' y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
152	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
153	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N)
154	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl P) _ _) _)
155	
156	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
157	lemma toOpen_map_app {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
158	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)) :
159	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.map (tilde.map f)).1.app _ =
160	    f ≫ toOpen N U := by
161	  ext x; exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ IsLocalizedModule.map_apply y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
162	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
163	     (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N) _ x)
164	
165	variable (R) in
166	/-- Tilde as a functor -/
167	@[simps] protected noncomputable def functor : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec (.of R)).Modules where
168	  obj := tilde
169	  map := tilde.map
170	
171	instance isIso_toOpen_top {M : ModuleCat R} : IsIso (toOpen M ⊤) := by
172	  rw [toOpen, isIso_comp_right_iff, ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
173	  exact StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_top_bijective
174	
175	/-- The isomorphism between the global sections of `M^~` and `M`. -/
176	@[simps! hom]
177	noncomputable def isoTop (M : ModuleCat R) :
178	    M ≅ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) :=
179	  asIso (toOpen M ⊤)

182	lemma isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) (f : R) :
183	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
184	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op (basicOpen f)))) f) := by
185	  rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
186	  change Function.Bijective (algebraMap Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f)
187	      (Module.End Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f) Γ(M, basicOpen f)) (algebraMap R _ f))

189	  exact (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _).map _

194	/-- This is the counit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
195	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) :
196	    tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) ⟶ M :=
197	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage
198	    ⟨TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ isBasis_basic_opens
199	    { app (f : Rᵒᵖ) := by
200	        refine (ModuleCat.ofHom (IsLocalizedModule.lift (.powers (M := R) f.unop)
201	          (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f.unop)).hom
202	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom ?_):)
203	        rw [Subtype.forall]
204	        change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
205	        simp only [inducedFunctor_obj, Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap]
206	        exact tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f.unop
207	      naturality {f g : Rᵒᵖ} i := by
208	        letI N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)
209	        ext1

214	          simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
215	          obtain ⟨n, a, e⟩ : ∃ n, f.unop ∣ g.unop ^ n := by

316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }

322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=

332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)

339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)

13	/-!
14	# Sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings
15	
16	In this file, we define the category `SheafOfModules R` when `R : Sheaf J RingCat`
17	is a sheaf of rings on a category `C` equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.
18	
19	-/
20	
21	@[expose] public section
22	
23	universe v v₁ u₁ u w
24	
25	open CategoryTheory
26	
27	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
28	  (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u})
29	
30	/-- A sheaf of modules is a presheaf of modules such that the underlying presheaf
31	of abelian groups is a sheaf. -/
32	structure SheafOfModules where
33	  /-- the underlying presheaf of modules of a sheaf of modules -/
34	  val : PresheafOfModules.{v} R.obj
35	  isSheaf : Presheaf.IsSheaf J val.presheaf
36	
37	namespace SheafOfModules
38	
39	variable {R}
40	
41	/-- A morphism between sheaves of modules is a morphism between the underlying
42	presheaves of modules. -/
43	@[ext]
44	structure Hom (X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
45	  /-- a morphism between the underlying presheaves of modules -/
46	  val : X.val ⟶ Y.val
47	
48	instance : Category (SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
49	  Hom := Hom
50	  id _ := ⟨𝟙 _⟩
51	  comp f g := ⟨f.val ≫ g.val⟩
52	
53	@[ext]
54	lemma hom_ext {X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f.val = g.val) : f = g :=
55	  Hom.ext h
56	
57	@[simp]
58	lemma id_val (X : SheafOfModules.{v} R) : Hom.val (𝟙 X) = 𝟙 X.val := rfl
59	
60	@[simp, reassoc]
61	lemma comp_val {X Y Z : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
62	    (f ≫ g).val = f.val ≫ g.val := rfl
63	
64	variable (R)
65	/-- The forgetful functor `SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.val`. -/
66	@[simps]
67	def forget : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.obj where
68	  obj F := F.val
69	  map φ := φ.val

71	/-- The forget functor `SheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.val` is fully faithful. -/
72	@[simps]

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.DirectSum.Finsupp

12	/-! # Base change of a free module
13	
14	* `IsBaseChange.basis` : the natural basis of the base change of a module with a basis
15	
16	* `IsBaseChange.free` : a base change of a free module is free.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	namespace IsBaseChange
23	
24	variable {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R]
25	    {S : Type*} [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S]
26	    {V : Type*} [AddCommMonoid V] [Module R V]
27	    {W : Type*} [AddCommMonoid W] [Module R W] [Module S W] [IsScalarTower R S W]
28	    {ι : Type*}
29	    {ε : V →ₗ[R] W}
30	
31	variable (b : Module.Basis ι R V) (ibc : IsBaseChange S ε)
32	
33	/-- The basis of a module deduced by base change from a free module with a basis. -/
34	noncomputable def basis :
35	    Module.Basis ι S W where
36	  repr := (ibc.equiv.symm.trans (b.repr.baseChange R S _ _)).trans
37	      (finsuppPow ι (linearMap R S)).equiv
38	
39	theorem basis_apply (i) : ibc.basis b i = ε (b i) := by
40	  simp only [basis, LinearEquiv.baseChange, Module.Basis.coe_ofRepr, LinearEquiv.trans_symm,
41	    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, LinearEquiv.trans_apply]
42	  generalize_proofs _ _ _ _ ibcRA
43	  have : ibcRA.equiv.symm (Finsupp.single i 1) = 1 ⊗ₜ (Finsupp.single i 1) := by
44	    simp [LinearEquiv.symm_apply_eq, IsBaseChange.equiv_tmul]
45	  simp [this, IsBaseChange.equiv_tmul]
46	
47	theorem basis_repr_comp_apply (v i) :
48	    (ibc.basis b).repr (ε v) i = algebraMap R S (b.repr v i) := by
49	  conv_lhs => rw [← b.linearCombination_repr v, Finsupp.linearCombination_apply,
50	    map_finsuppSum, map_finsuppSum]
51	  simp only [map_smul, Finsupp.sum_apply]
52	  rw [Finsupp.sum_eq_single i]
53	  · rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul S (b.repr v i) (ε (b i)),
54	      map_smul, ← ibc.basis_apply]
55	    simp [Finsupp.single_eq_same, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
56	  · intro i' _ h
57	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul S (b.repr v i') (ε (b i')), map_smul,
58	      ← ibc.basis_apply]
59	    simp [Finsupp.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm h)]
60	  · simp
61	
62	theorem basis_repr_comp (v : V) :
63	    (ibc.basis b).repr (ε v) =
64	      Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap R S) (b.repr v) := by
65	  ext i
66	  simp [basis_repr_comp_apply]
67	
68	include ibc in
69	theorem free [Module.Free R V] : Module.Free S W :=
70	  Module.Free.of_basis (ibc.basis (Module.Free.chooseBasis R V))
71	
72	end IsBaseChange
73	
74	section underring
75	
76	namespace IsBaseChange
77	
78	open TensorProduct
79	
80	variable {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R]
81	  {V : Type*} [AddCommMonoid V] [Module R V]
82	  (A : Type*) [CommSemiring A] [Algebra A R]
83	  [Module A V] [IsScalarTower A R V]
84	
85	open TensorProduct
86	
87	variable {ι : Type*} (b : Module.Basis ι R V)
88	
89	theorem of_basis : IsBaseChange R (Finsupp.linearCombination A b) := by
90	  classical
91	  let j := TensorProduct.finsuppScalarRight A R R ι
92	  refine of_equiv ?_ ?_
93	  · apply LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Finsupp.linearCombination R b ∘ₗ j)
94	    rw [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, j.bijective.of_comp_iff]
95	    simp [Function.Bijective,
96	        ← span_range_eq_top_iff_surjective_finsuppLinearCombination,
97	        ← linearIndependent_iff_injective_finsuppLinearCombination,
98	        Module.Basis.span_eq, b.linearIndependent]
99	  · intro x
100	    suffices (j (1 ⊗ₜ[A] x)) = x.mapRange (algebraMap A R) (by simp) by
101	      simp [this, Finsupp.linearCombination_apply, Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index]
102	    ext i
103	    simp [j, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
104	
105	include A in
106	/-- Any finite basis of a module can express it as the base change
107	of a finite free module from any under-ring. -/
108	theorem of_fintype_basis [Fintype ι] :
109	    IsBaseChange R (Fintype.linearCombination A b) := by
110	  have : DecidableEq ι := Classical.typeDecidableEq ι
111	  let j : R ⊗[A] (ι → A) ≃ₗ[R] ι → R := piScalarRight A R R ι
112	  refine of_equiv ?_ ?_
113	  · apply LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Fintype.linearCombination R b ∘ₗ j)
114	    rw [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, j.bijective.of_comp_iff]
115	    simp [Function.Bijective,
116	        ← span_range_eq_top_iff_surjective_fintypeLinearCombination,
117	        ← linearIndependent_iff_injective_fintypeLinearCombination,
118	        Module.Basis.span_eq, b.linearIndependent]
119	  · intro x
120	    -- simp? [Fintype.linearCombination_apply] says:
121	    simp only [LinearEquiv.ofBijective_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
122	      Function.comp_apply, Fintype.linearCombination_apply]
123	    congr
124	    ext i
125	    rw [TensorProduct.piScalarRight_apply, TensorProduct.piScalarRightHom_tmul]
126	    simp
127	
128	variable {A b} in
129	theorem of_fintype_basis_eq [Fintype ι] {a : ι → A} {v : V} :
130	    (Fintype.linearCombination A b) a = v ↔
131	      algebraMap A R ∘ a = b.equivFun v := by
132	  rw [← LinearEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
133	  rw [Fintype.linearCombination_apply, b.equivFun_symm_apply]
134	  simp
135	
136	end IsBaseChange
137	
138	end underring

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Pi
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Prod
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.BaseChange
11	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.Finite.Basic
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangeFree
13	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Determinant
14	
15	/-! # Base change properties for modules of linear maps
16	
17	* `IsBaseChange.linearMapRight`:
18	  If `M` is finite free and `P` is a base change of `N` to `S`,
19	  then `M →ₗ[R] P` is a base change of `M →ₗ[R] N` to `S`.
20	
21	* `IsBaseChange.linearMapLeftRight`:
22	  If `M` is finite free and `P` is a base change of `M` to `S`,
23	  if `Q` is a base change of `N` to `S`,
24	  then `P →ₗ[S] Q` is a base change of `M →ₗ[R] N` to `S`.
25	
26	* `IsBaseChange.end`:
27	  If `M` is finite free and `P` is a base change of `M` to `S`,
28	  then `P →ₗ[S] P` is a base change of `M →ₗ[R] M` to `S`.
29	
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public section
33	
34	namespace IsBaseChange
35	
36	open LinearMap TensorProduct Module
37	
38	variable {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R]
39	    (S : Type*) [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S]
40	    (M : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
41	    {N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]
42	    {P : Type*} [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P]
43	
44	section LinearMapRight
45	
46	variable [Module S P] [IsScalarTower R S P]
47	
48	/-- The base change homomorphism underlying `IsBaseChange.linearMapRight` -/
49	def linearMapRightBaseChangeHom (ε : N →ₗ[R] P) :
50	    (S ⊗[R] (M →ₗ[R] N)) →ₗ[S] (M →ₗ[R] P) where
51	  toAddHom := (TensorProduct.lift {
52	    toFun s := s • (LinearMap.compRight R ε (M := M))
53	    map_add' x y := by ext; simp [add_smul]
54	    map_smul' r s := by simp }).toAddHom
55	  map_smul' s x := by
56	    simp only [AddHom.toFun_eq_coe, coe_toAddHom, RingHom.id_apply]
57	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
58	    | zero => simp
59	    | add x y hx hy => simp [smul_add, hx, hy]
60	    | tmul t f => simp [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', mul_smul]
61	
62	variable [Free R M] [Module.Finite R M]
63	
64	variable {S}
65	
66	/-- The base change isomorphism underlying `IsBaseChange.linearMapRight` -/
67	noncomputable def linearMapRightBaseChangeEquiv
68	    {ε : N →ₗ[R] P} (ibc : IsBaseChange S ε) :
69	    S ⊗[R] (M →ₗ[R] N) ≃ₗ[S] (M →ₗ[R] P) := by
70	  apply LinearEquiv.ofBijective (linearMapRightBaseChangeHom S M ε)
71	  let b := Free.chooseBasis R M
72	  set ι := Free.ChooseBasisIndex R M
73	  have := Free.ChooseBasisIndex.fintype R M
74	  let e := (b.repr.congrLeft N R).trans (Finsupp.llift N R R ι).symm
75	  let f := (b.repr.congrLeft P S).trans (Finsupp.llift P R S ι).symm
76	  let h := linearMapRightBaseChangeHom S M ε
77	  let e' : S ⊗[R] (M →ₗ[R] N) ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] (ι → N) :=
78	    LinearEquiv.baseChange R S (M →ₗ[R] N) (ι → N) e
79	  let h' := (f.toLinearMap.comp (linearMapRightBaseChangeHom S M ε)).comp e'.symm.toLinearMap
80	  suffices Function.Bijective h' by simpa [h'] using this
81	  suffices h' = (finitePow ι ibc).equiv by
82	    simp only [this]
83	    apply LinearEquiv.bijective
84	  suffices f.toLinearMap.comp (linearMapRightBaseChangeHom S M ε) =
85	      (finitePow ι ibc).equiv.toLinearMap.comp e'.toLinearMap by
86	    simp [h', this, ← LinearEquiv.trans_assoc e'.symm e']
87	  ext φ i
88	  simp
89	  simp [f, e', linearMapRightBaseChangeHom, LinearEquiv.baseChange, equiv_tmul,
90	    LinearEquiv.congrLeft, e]
91	
92	/-- If `M` has a finite basis and `P` is a base change of `N` to `S`,
93	then `M →ₗ[R] P` is a base change of `M →ₗ[R] N` to `S`. -/
94	theorem linearMapRight {ε : N →ₗ[R] P} (ibc : IsBaseChange S ε) :
95	    IsBaseChange S (LinearMap.compRight (M := M) R ε) := by
96	  apply of_equiv (linearMapRightBaseChangeEquiv M ibc)
97	  intro f
98	  simp [linearMapRightBaseChangeEquiv, linearMapRightBaseChangeHom]
99	
100	end LinearMapRight
101	
102	section LinearMapLeftRight
103	
104	variable {S M}
105	  {Q : Type*} [AddCommMonoid Q] [Module R Q]
106	  [Module S P] [IsScalarTower R S P]
107	  [Module S Q] [IsScalarTower R S Q]
108	
109	/-- The base change map for linear maps with source a free finite module. -/
110	noncomputable def linearMapLeftRightHom {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α)
111	    (β : N →ₗ[R] Q) :
112	    (M →ₗ[R] N) →ₗ[R] (P →ₗ[S] Q) :=
113	  ((LinearMap.llcomp (σ₂₃ := RingHom.id S) S P (S ⊗[R] M) Q).flip
114	    j.equiv.symm.toLinearMap) ∘ₗ
115	    (liftBaseChangeEquiv S).toLinearMap.restrictScalars R ∘ₗ
116	      (compRight R β (M := M))
117	
118	theorem linearMapLeftRightHom_apply
119	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (β : N →ₗ[R] Q) (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (p : P) :
120	    linearMapLeftRightHom j β f p = ((liftBaseChangeEquiv S) (β ∘ₗ f)) (j.equiv.symm p) := by
121	  rfl
122	
123	@[simp] theorem linearMapLeftRightHom_comp_apply
124	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (β : N →ₗ[R] Q) (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (m : M) :
125	    linearMapLeftRightHom j β f (α m) = β (f m) := by
126	  simp [linearMapLeftRightHom_apply, IsBaseChange.equiv_symm_apply]
127	
128	@[simp] theorem linearMapLeftRightHom_comp
129	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (β : N →ₗ[R] Q) (f : M →ₗ[R] N) :
130	    (linearMapLeftRightHom j β f).restrictScalars R ∘ₗ α = β ∘ₗ f := by
131	  ext; simp [linearMapLeftRightHom_comp_apply]
132	
133	variable [Free R M] [Module.Finite R M]
134	
135	theorem linearMapLeftRight {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α)
136	    {β : N →ₗ[R] Q} (k : IsBaseChange S β) :
137	    IsBaseChange S (linearMapLeftRightHom j β) := by
138	  apply of_equiv <|
139	      (k.linearMapRight M).equiv ≪≫ₗ liftBaseChangeEquiv S ≪≫ₗ LinearEquiv.congrLeft Q S j.equiv
140	  intro f
141	  ext p
142	  simp [IsBaseChange.equiv_tmul, LinearEquiv.congrLeft, linearMapLeftRightHom_apply]
143	
144	end LinearMapLeftRight
145	
146	section End
147	
148	variable {S M}
149	  [Module S P] [IsScalarTower R S P]
150	
151	/-- The base change map for endomorphisms of a free finite module. -/
152	noncomputable def endHom {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) :
153	    (M →ₗ[R] M) →ₗ[R] (P →ₗ[S] P) :=
154	  ((LinearMap.llcomp (σ₂₃ := RingHom.id S) S P (S ⊗[R] M) P).flip
155	    j.equiv.symm.toLinearMap) ∘ₗ
156	    (liftBaseChangeEquiv S).toLinearMap.restrictScalars R ∘ₗ
157	      (compRight R α (M := M))
158	
159	theorem endHom_apply
160	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (f : M →ₗ[R] M) (p : P) :
161	    endHom j f p = ((liftBaseChangeEquiv S) (α ∘ₗ f)) (j.equiv.symm p) := by
162	  rfl
163	
164	theorem endHom_comp_apply
165	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (f : M →ₗ[R] M) (m : M) :
166	    endHom j f (α m) = α (f m) := by
167	  simp [endHom_apply, IsBaseChange.equiv_symm_apply]
168	
169	theorem endHom_comp
170	    {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) (f : M →ₗ[R] M) :
171	    (endHom j f).restrictScalars R ∘ₗ α = α ∘ₗ f := by
172	  ext; simp [endHom_comp_apply]
173	
174	theorem endHom_one {α : M →ₗ[R] P} (j : IsBaseChange S α) :
175	    j.endHom 1 = 1 := by
176	  ext p
177	  induction p using j.inductionOn with
178	  | zero => simp
179	  | add x y hx hy => simp [hx, hy]
180	  | smul _ _ h => simp [h]
181	  | tmul m => simp [endHom_comp_apply]
182	
183	variable [Free R M] [Module.Finite R M]
184

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PushforwardContinuous
11	
12	/-!
13	# Pullback of sheaves of modules
14	
15	Let `S` and `R` be sheaves of rings over sites `(C, J)` and `(D, K)` respectively.
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a continuous functor between these sites, and
17	let `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` be a morphism
18	of sheaves of rings.
19	
20	In this file, we define the pullback functor for sheaves of modules
21	`pullback.{v} φ : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`
22	that is left adjoint to `pushforward.{v} φ`. We show that it exists
23	under suitable assumptions, and prove that the pullback of (pre)sheaves of
24	modules commutes with the sheafification.
25	
26	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
27	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
28	
29	-/
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Functor
36	
37	namespace SheafOfModules
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
40	  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
41	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
42	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
43	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
44	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
45	
46	section
47	
48	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
49	
50	/-- The pullback functor `SheafOfModules S ⥤ SheafOfModules R` induced by
51	a morphism of sheaves of rings `S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`,
52	defined as the left adjoint functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
53	noncomputable def pullback : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R :=
54	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
55	
56	/-- Given a continuous functor between sites `F`, and a morphism of sheaves of rings
57	`S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`, this is the adjunction
58	between the corresponding pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
59	of sheaves of modules. -/
60	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
61	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
62	
63	instance : (pullback.{v} φ).IsLeftAdjoint :=
64	  (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).isLeftAdjoint
65	
66	end
67	
68	section
69	
70	variable [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{v} φ.hom).IsRightAdjoint]
71	  [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
72	
73	namespace PullbackConstruction
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	/-- Construction of a left adjoint to the functor `pushforward.{v} φ` by using the
77	pullback of presheaves of modules and the sheafification. -/
78	noncomputable def adjunction :
79	    (forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
80	      PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj)) ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
81	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
82	    { homEquiv := fun F G ↦
83	        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).homEquiv _ _).trans
84	            (((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).homEquiv F.val G.val).trans
85	              ((fullyFaithfulForget S).homEquiv (Y := (pushforward φ).obj G)).symm)
86	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := by
87	        intros
88	        dsimp [Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv]
89	        -- these erw seem difficult to remove
90	        erw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
91	          Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
92	        dsimp [pushforward_obj_val]
93	        simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
94	      homEquiv_naturality_right := by
95	        tauto }
96	
97	end PullbackConstruction
98	
99	instance : (pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
100	  (PullbackConstruction.adjunction.{v} φ).isRightAdjoint
101	
102	/-- The pullback functor on sheaves of modules can be described as a composition
103	of the forget functor to presheaves, the pullback on presheaves of modules, and
104	the sheafification functor. -/
105	noncomputable def pullbackIso :
106	    pullback.{v} φ ≅
107	      forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
108	        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
109	  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
110	    (PullbackConstruction.adjunction φ)
111	
112	section
113	
114	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
115	
116	/-- The pullback of (pre)sheaves of modules commutes with the sheafification. -/
117	noncomputable def sheafificationCompPullback :
118	    PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.obj) ⋙ pullback.{v} φ ≅
119	      PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
120	        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
121	  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
122	    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 S.obj)).comp
123	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ))
124	    ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).comp
125	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)))
126	
127	end
128	
129	end
130	
131	
132	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
133	  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId S).symm
134	
135	variable (S) in
136	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
137	category of sheaves of modules. -/
138	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
139	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
140	
141	variable (S) in
142	@[simp]
143	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
144	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} _) (pullbackId S).hom =
145	      (pushforwardId S).inv :=
146	  Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointIdIso_hom _ _
147	
148	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
149	
150	section

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Free
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Shapes.Products
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final.Type
12	
13	/-!
14	# Pullbacks of free sheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `S` (resp.`R`) be a sheaf of rings on a category `C` (resp. `D`)
17	equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` (resp. `K`).
18	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a continuous functor.
19	Let `φ` be a morphism from `S` to the direct image of `R`.
20	
21	We introduce `unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ` which is the morphism
22	in the category `SheafOfModules S` which corresponds to `φ`, and
23	show that the adjoint morphism
24	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ : (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
25	is an isomorphism when `F` is a final functor.
26	More generally, the functor `pullback φ` sends the free sheaf
27	of modules `free I` to `free I`, see `pullbackObjFreeIso` and
28	`freeFunctorCompPullbackIso`.
29	-/
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	universe v v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂ u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace SheafOfModules
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
40	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
41	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
42	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
43	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
44	
45	/-- The canonical map from the (global) sections of a sheaf of modules
46	to the (global) sections of its pushforward. -/
47	@[simps]
48	def pushforwardSections {M : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) :
49	    ((pushforward φ).obj M).sections where
50	  val _ := s.val _
51	  property _ := s.property _
52	
53	variable (M) in
54	lemma bijective_pushforwardSections [F.Final] :
55	    Function.Bijective (pushforwardSections φ (M := M)) :=
56	  Functor.bijective_sectionsPrecomp _ _
57	
58	variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
59	  [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
60	
61	/-- The canonical morphism `unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R)`
62	of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
63	noncomputable def unitToPushforwardObjUnit : unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R) where
64	  val.app X := ModuleCat.homMk ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (φ.hom.app X)) (fun r ↦ by
65	    ext m
66	    exact ((φ.hom.app X).hom.map_mul _ _).symm)
67	  val.naturality f := by
68	    ext
69	    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (φ.hom.naturality f) _
70	
71	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj.obj X) :
72	    (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val.app X a = φ.hom.app X a := rfl
73	
74	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
75	lemma pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv
76	    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : unit R ⟶ M) :
77	    pushforwardSections φ (M.unitHomEquiv f) =
78	      ((pushforward φ).obj M).unitHomEquiv
79	        (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ ≫ (pushforward φ).map f) := by
80	  ext X
81	  have := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply φ (X := X) 1
82	  dsimp at this ⊢
83	  simp +instances [this, map_one]
84	  rfl
85	
86	variable [(pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
87	
88	/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
89	of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
90	noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
91	    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
92	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)
93	
94	@[simp]
95	lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
96	    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
97	      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl
98	
99	@[simp]
100	lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
101	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
102	      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
103	  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
104	
105	instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
106	  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
107	  intro M
108	  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
109	    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
110	  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
111	  ext f : 1
112	  dsimp
113	  rw [pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
114	    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
115	    pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]
116	
117	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
118	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
119	  [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [F.Final]
120	
121	/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
122	noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
123	    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
124	  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
125	    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
129	lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
130	    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
131	      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
132	  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]
133	
134	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
135	lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
136	    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
137	      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
138	  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
139	    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])
140	
141	/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
142	continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
143	is final. -/
144	noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
145	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)
146	
147	end SheafOfModules
