3	\chapter{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)}
4	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}
5	
6	% SOURCE: Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, IV \S1, pp.~294--297
7	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages
8	% 311--314). The verbatim quotes pasted in the declaration blocks below
9	% were copied character-by-character from the rendered page images of
10	% the scanned PDF (no text layer is present).
11	
12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	
14	This chapter is the second of four sub-build chapters \texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4}
15	that close the project's headline RR bridge
16	\[
17	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
18	  \,\colon\,
19	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
20	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
21	\]
22	The bridge consumed by
23	REF of
24	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex} is the Hartshorne~IV.1.3.5 chain:
25	the Weil-divisor group \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) and the degree map $\deg \colon \mathrm{Div}(C)
26	\to \mathbb Z$ (\texttt{RR.1}, REF); the
27	Riemann--Roch dimension formula in genus~\(0\)
28	\[
29	  \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0
30	\]
31	(this chapter, \texttt{RR.2}); the global-sections identification
32	\(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\) (\texttt{RR.3}); the
33	``rational curve \(\Rightarrow \cong \mathbb P^1\)'' classification via
34	\texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} (\texttt{RR.4}).
35	
36	% NOTE (Serre duality vs. direct \(\chi\) — iter-173 sub-phase choice).
37	% This chapter proves the RR formula via the \emph{direct \(\chi\)
38	% inductive proof} (Hartshorne IV.1.3 step ``Next, let \(D\) be any
39	% divisor\ldots'', p.~296), \emph{not} via Serre duality. The reason is
40	% strictly Mathlib-side: Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships no
41	% dualizing sheaf \(\omega_X\) on a scheme and no Serre duality theorem
42	% \(H^i(X, \mathcal F) \cong H^{n-i}(X, \omega_X \otimes \mathcal F^\vee)^*\);
43	% routing through that machinery would force a multi-iter sub-build
44	% strictly heavier than the genus-\(0\) critical path requires. The
45	% direct \(\chi\) proof depends only on additivity of the Euler
46	% characteristic on a short exact sequence of coherent sheaves (a
47	% standard preadditive-category fact), the genus base case
48	% \(\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g\), and the closed-point skyscraper
49	% identity \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\).
50	
51	% NOTE (Genus-\(0\) carve-out vs. general \(g\) — iter-173 sub-phase
52	% choice). The chapter restricts the headline theorem
53	% REF to the genus-\(0\) specialisation
54	% \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\). The general-\(g\)
55	% statement \(\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) is downstream of
56	% \texttt{RR.2} (it requires the canonical divisor \(K\) and Serre
57	% duality / the \(K - D\) pairing) and is \emph{not} needed for the
58	% genus-\(0\) critical path: \texttt{RR.3} consumes only
59	% REF, and \texttt{RR.4} consumes
60	% \texttt{RR.3}. The intermediate \(\chi\)-identity
61	% REF is stated and
62	% sketched for general \(g\) (the inductive argument is genus-uniform);
63	% its full closure on a general-\(g\) curve waits for the project's
64	% subsequent Riemann--Roch chapter, but the proof body in
65	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean} pins the
66	% \emph{statement} for general \(g\) and may close the body initially
67	% under a \(g = 0\) hypothesis.
68	
69	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, \(\bar k\) is an
70	algebraically closed field and \(C\) is a smooth proper geometrically
71	irreducible curve over \(\bar k\). Concretely, the Lean signatures
72	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean}) carry the same
73	typeclass package as the rest of the project's curve API:
74	\begin{quote}
75	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
76	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
77	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
78	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]}
79	\end{quote}
80	The genus \(g(C)\) is the one defined in REF of
81	\texttt{Genus.tex}, namely \(g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\)
82	via the project's \(\Module\bar k\)-flavoured sheaf cohomology
83	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}).
84	
85	\section{The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve}
86	
87	\begin{definition}
88	
89	
90	\leanok
91	[Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve]
92	  \label{def:eulerChar_curve}
93	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic}
94	  
95	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, p.~295 (definition of \(\chi(\mathcal F)\)
96	  % inside the proof of Theorem~1.3) (read from
97	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
98	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any \(D\),
99	  % \(\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g\),
100	  % where for any coherent sheaf \(\mathscr F\) on \(X\), \(\chi(\mathscr F)\)
101	  % is the Euler characteristic
102	  % \(\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)\)."
103	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~295, the formula displayed
104	  inside the proof of Theorem~1.3.}
105	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let \(\mathcal F\)
106	  be a coherent sheaf on \(C\). The \emph{Euler characteristic} of
107	  \(\mathcal F\) is the integer
108	  \[
109	    \chi(\mathcal F) \;:=\;
110	    \sum_{i \geq 0} (-1)^i \,\dim_{\bar k} H^i(C, \mathcal F)
111	    \;=\;
112	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal F)
113	    \,-\,
114	    \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal F).
115	  \]
116	  The second equality is a curve-specific simplification: because
117	  \(\dim C = 1\), Grothendieck vanishing (Hartshorne~III.2.7) gives
118	  \(H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0\) for \(i \geq 2\), so the alternating sum has
119	  only two terms. Coherence of \(\mathcal F\) on the proper $\bar
120	  k\(-scheme \)C\( guarantees that both \)H^0\( and \)H^1$ are
121	  finite-dimensional \(\bar k\)-vector spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology
122	  finiteness theorem, the same finiteness backing
123	  REF), so the two terms are honest natural numbers and
124	  the difference is a well-defined integer.
125	
126	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
127	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic} takes a coherent
128	  sheaf \(\mathcal F\) on \(C\) and returns an integer; the natural input
129	  shape is the project's \(\Module \bar k\)-valued sheaf-cohomology
130	  wrapper \(\HModule \bar k\) (REF) applied to a
131	  \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured carrier built from \(\mathcal F\), with
132	  \(\dim_{\bar k}\) extracted via \(\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\). The
133	  declaration is \texttt{noncomputable} (inherited from
134	  \(\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\) and from the \(\Ext\)-based cohomology
135	  construction).
136	\end{definition}
137	
138	\section{The \(\ell\)-invariant of a Weil divisor}
139	
140	\begin{definition}
141	
142	
143	\leanok
144	[\(\ell\)-invariant of a Weil divisor]
145	  \label{def:l_invariant}
146	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l}
147	  
148	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, p.~295 (definition of \(l(D)\) and of the
149	  % "dimension of \(|D|\)") (read from
150	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
151	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We denote \(\dim_k H^0(X, \mathscr L(D))\) by \(l(D)\),
152	  % so that the \emph{dimension of \(|D|\)} is \(l(D) - 1\). The number
153	  % \(l(D)\) is finite by (II, 5.19) or (III, 5.2)."
154	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, p.~295 (definition of \(l(D)\)).}
155	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\) and let
156	  \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) be a Weil divisor (REF). The
157	  \emph{\(\ell\)-invariant of \(D\)} is the integer
158	  \[
159	    \ell(D) \;:=\; \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
160	    \;\in\; \mathbb N,
161	  \]
162	  the \(\bar k\)-dimension of the space of global sections of the
163	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) associated to \(D\). Finiteness of
164	  \(\ell(D)\) is a consequence of the coherent-cohomology finiteness
165	  theorem on a proper \(\bar k\)-scheme (Hartshorne~II.5.19 or
166	  III.5.2). The classical \emph{complete linear system \(|D|\)} has
167	  \(\bar k\)-dimension \(\ell(D) - 1\) as a projective space, but the
168	  chapter never uses the projective-space interpretation; \(\ell(D)\) is
169	  the only quantity it needs.
170	
171	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope.} The Lean definition
172	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l} accepts a divisor
173	  \(D : C.\mathtt{WeilDivisor}\) (REF) and returns
174	  \(\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\;\bar k\;(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D)))\),
175	  computed through the same \(\Module \bar k\)-valued sheaf-cohomology
176	  pipeline as REF. The associated invertible
177	  sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) is the line bundle of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
178	  chapter, to be added); the present chapter's prose and Lean
179	  signature reference \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) by name and consume only its
180	  \(H^0\) functor. The declaration is \texttt{noncomputable}.
181	\end{definition}
182	
183	\section{The \(\chi\) identity: \(\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\)}
184	
185	\begin{theorem}
186	
187	
188	\leanok
189	[Euler-characteristic identity for \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)]
190	  \label{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus}
191	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus}
192	  
193	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Theorem~1.3 (the reduction inside the
194	  % proof: ``Thus we have to show that for any \(D\), $\chi(\mathscr L(D))
195	  % = \deg D + 1 - g$'') (read from
196	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
197	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Theorem 1.3 (Riemann--Roch). \emph{Let \(D\) be a
198	  % divisor on a curve \(X\) of genus \(g\). Then}
199	  % \(l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g\)."
200	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any \(D\),
201	  % \(\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g\),
202	  % where for any coherent sheaf \(\mathscr F\) on \(X\), \(\chi(\mathscr F)\)
203	  % is the Euler characteristic
204	  % \(\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)\)."
205	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Theorem~1.3, p.~295.}
206	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
207	  \(\bar k\) of genus \(g = g(C)\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\). Then
208	  \[
209	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g.
210	  \]
211	\end{theorem}
212	
213	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "First we consider the case \(D = 0\). Then our
214	% formula says
215	% \(\dim H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = 1 - g\),
216	% because \(H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) = k\) for any projective variety
217	% (I, 3.4), and \(\dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = g\) by (1.1).
218	% Next, let \(D\) be any divisor, and let \(P\) be any point. We will show
219	% that the formula is true for \(D\) if and only if it is true for
220	% \(D + P\). Since any divisor can be reached from \(0\) in a finite number
221	% of steps by adding or subtracting a point each time, this will show
222	% the result holds for all \(D\).
223	% We consider \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(X\). Its structure sheaf is
224	% the skyscraper sheaf \(k\) sitting at the point \(P\), which we denote by
225	% \(k(P)\), and its ideal sheaf is \(\mathscr L(-P)\) by (II, 6.18).
226	% Therefore we have an exact sequence
227	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0\).
228	% Tensoring with \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) we get
229	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\),
230	% (since \(\mathscr L(P)\) is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by it
231	% does not affect the sheaf \(k(P)\)). Now the Euler characteristic is
232	% additive on short exact sequences (III, Ex. 5.1), and $\chi(k(P))
233	% = 1$, so we have
234	% \(\chi(\mathscr L(D + P)) = \chi(\mathscr L(D)) + 1\).
235	% On the other hand, \(\deg D + P = \deg D + 1\), so our formula is true
236	% for \(D\) if and only if it is true for \(D + P\), as required."
237	\begin{proof}
238	  \leanok
239	  
240	  The proof is by induction on the structure of \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as the free
241	  abelian group on closed points (REF,
242	  REF). Both sides of the asserted
243	  equation are additive in \(D\) (the right-hand side by linearity of
244	  \(\deg\), REF; the left-hand side by the
245	  inductive step proved below), so it suffices to verify the formula
246	  for \(D = 0\) and to show that it transports across the elementary
247	  modification \(D \mapsto D + [P]\) at any closed point \(P \in C\).
248	
249	  \emph{Base case \(D = 0\).} The line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(0)\) is the
250	  structure sheaf \(\mathcal O_C\), and the formula
251	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g\) reads
252	  \[
253	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
254	    - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)
255	    \;=\; 1 - g.
256	  \]
257	  The second term is the definition of the genus,
258	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = g\) (REF). The
259	  first term is \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = 1\): \(C\) is a
260	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over the algebraically
261	  closed field \(\bar k\), so its connected components agree with its
262	  geometrically connected components, and $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
263	  \cong \bar k\( as \)\bar k$-vector spaces (the Hartshorne~I.3.4
264	  statement that ``\(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X) = k\) for any projective
265	  variety'' over an algebraically closed field; the Mathlib home is
266	  the project's \(H^0\)-bridge in
267	  \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex}). Substituting these
268	  two values gives \(1 - g = 1 - g\), as required.
269	
270	  \emph{Inductive step (Hartshorne's ``\(D \leftrightsquigarrow D + P\)''
271	  step).} Let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. We follow Hartshorne's
272	  argument: view \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(C\); its structure
273	  sheaf is the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P) \cong \bar k\) supported at \(P\),
274	  and its sheaf of ideals is \(\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C\)
275	  (the locally principal ideal sheaf cutting out \(P\) in \(C\)). Hence
276	  the short exact sequence
277	  \[
278	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \to \mathcal O_C \to k(P) \to 0
279	  \]
280	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\) (Hartshorne~II.6.18). Tensoring with the
281	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) (a locally free
282	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-module of rank \(1\), so tensoring preserves exactness
283	  and leaves the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) unchanged) yields
284	  \[
285	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(D)
286	    \to \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
287	    \to k(P) \to 0.
288	  \]
289	  Additivity of the Euler characteristic on a short exact sequence
290	  of coherent sheaves (Mathlib's preadditive-category Euler
291	  characteristic; see the Lean reference note at the end of this
292	  proof) gives
293	  \[
294	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P]))
295	    \;=\;
296	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + \chi(k(P)).
297	  \]
298	  The skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) has
299	  \(H^0(C, k(P)) \cong \bar k\) and \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\) (the cohomology
300	  of a flasque sheaf supported at a closed point is concentrated in
301	  degree \(0\), with \(H^0\) equal to the stalk \(\bar k\)), so
302	  \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\). Therefore
303	  \[
304	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])) \;=\; \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + 1.
305	  \]
306	  On the other hand, \(\deg([P]) = 1\) (every closed point contributes
307	  \(1\) to the bare-sum degree of REF), so by
308	  REF
309	  \[
310	    \deg(D + [P]) + 1 - g \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g + 1.
311	  \]
312	  Comparing the two displayed identities, the formula
313	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) holds for \(D\) if and only
314	  if it holds for \(D + [P]\). Since the closed-point divisors \([P]\)
315	  generate \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as a free abelian group, the formula propagates
316	  from \(D = 0\) to every \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) by induction on the (signed)
317	  number of generators in the expression of \(D\).
318	
319	  \emph{Lean reference note on \(\chi\)-additivity.} Mathlib (snapshot
320	  \texttt{b80f227}) provides the Euler-characteristic API
321	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.EulerCharacteristic}
322	  (\texttt{HomologicalComplex.eulerChar}, \texttt{GradedObject.eulerChar})
323	  but does \emph{not} ship the specific additivity statement
324	  \texttt{CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar\_additive} [expected,
325	  not in Mathlib under that name]. The genus-\(0\) proof of
326	  REF therefore threads additivity of
327	  \(\chi\) on a short exact sequence either as a project-side lemma
328	  proved inside \texttt{RRFormula.lean} (from
329	  \texttt{Submodule.finrank\_quotient\_add\_finrank} combined with
330	  the long exact sequence of \(H^*\)) or as a candidate Mathlib upstream
331	  PR. The base case \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\) is closed by the Mathlib
332	  skyscraper-sheaf API together with the project's \(H^0\)-bridge
333	  (REF).
334	\end{proof}
335	
336	\section{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero}
337	
338	\begin{theorem}
339	
340	
341	\leanok
342	[Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero]
343	  \label{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}
344	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero}
345	  
346	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Example~1.3.5 (genus-\(0\) specialisation
347	  % of Riemann--Roch; the curve-is-rational consequence) (read from
348	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
349	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is \emph{rational}
350	  % if it is birational to \(\mathbf P^1\) (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in
351	  % this chapter are complete and nonsingular by definition, a curve
352	  % \(X\) is rational if and only if \(X \cong \mathbf P^1\) (I, 6.12). Now
353	  % using (1.3) we can show that \(X\) is rational if and only if \(g = 0\).
354	  % We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so
355	  % conversely suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two
356	  % distinct points of \(X\) and apply Riemann--Roch to the divisor
357	  % \(D = P - Q\). Since \(\deg K - D = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have
358	  % \(l(K - D) = 0\), and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of
359	  % degree 0, so by (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words $P \sim
360	  % Q\(. But this implies \)X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
361	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
362	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
363	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) with
364	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\). Then
365	  \[
366	    \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
367	  \]
368	\end{theorem}
369	
370	\begin{proof}
371	  \leanok
372	  
373	  Specialising
374	  REF to the genus-\(0\)
375	  hypothesis \(g = 0\) yields
376	  \[
377	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
378	  \]
379	  Unfolding \(\chi\) via REF and the definition
380	  \(\ell(D) = \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\)
381	  (REF), this rewrites as
382	  \[
383	    \ell(D) - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
384	    \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
385	  \]
386	  Under the standing genus-\(0\) hypothesis and the assumption
387	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\), the higher-cohomology term vanishes:
388	  \[
389	    H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; 0
390	    \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0.
391	  \]
392	  Substituting yields \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\), as required.
393	
394	  \emph{The \(H^1\)-vanishing input.} On a smooth proper geometrically
395	  irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), the vanishing
396	  \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\) is the Hartshorne
397	  formulation Lemma~1.2 read covariantly: \(\ell(D) \neq 0\) forces
398	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\); equivalently, for a curve where every
399	  degree-\(0\)-or-higher invertible sheaf is generated by global
400	  sections after a single point-twist, \(H^1\) of the twist must vanish
401	  by the long exact sequence of the skyscraper extension at any
402	  closed point. In the chain of sub-build chapters, this \(H^1\)
403	  vanishing is the natural content of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
404	  \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added), where the line
405	  bundle \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) and its cohomology are constructed; once
406	  \texttt{RR.3} is closed, the present theorem inherits the
407	  vanishing as an instance, and the proof closes verbatim as
408	  displayed. Pending \texttt{RR.3}, the Lean signature
409	  \texttt{l\_eq\_degree\_plus\_one\_of\_genus\_zero} threads the
410	  \(H^1\)-vanishing hypothesis explicitly as a named premise; the
411	  iteration that closes \texttt{RR.3} then drops the premise in a
412	  follow-up signature simplification.
413	
414	  \emph{Sub-build note for \texttt{RR.4}.} The downstream consumer of
415	  this theorem is the genus-\(0\) classification
416	  REF of
417	  \texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}: with \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\)
418	  and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity
419	  (REF), Hartshorne's Example~1.3.5 chain
420	  produces a degree-\(1\) morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via
421	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} applied to the two-dimensional
422	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\), which is then upgraded to an
423	  isomorphism in \texttt{RR.4}.
424	\end{proof}
425	
426	\section{Out of scope}
427	
428	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.2}
429	and are sequenced into the sibling sub-build chapters:
430	
431	\begin{itemize}
432	  \item The general-\(g\) Riemann--Roch theorem
433	    \(\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) (Hartshorne~IV.1.3 in
434	    its full form). This requires the canonical divisor \(K\), the
435	    canonical sheaf \(\Omega^1_{C / \bar k} = \omega_C\), and
436	    Serre duality $H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes
437	    \mathcal L^{-1})^*$. Mathlib has none of these for schemes, and
438	    the genus-\(0\) critical path does not require them; the general
439	    statement is queued for a follow-up chapter (post-RR-bridge).
440	  \item The construction of the invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)
441	    from a Weil divisor \(D\), the linear-equivalence isomorphism
442	    \(\mathcal O_C(D) \cong \mathcal O_C(D')\) when \(D \sim D'\), and
443	    the computation of $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \cong \bar k \cdot
444	    1 \oplus \bar k \cdot t$ (\emph{a non-constant rational function
445	    \(t\) exists}) live in \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
446	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added). Inside the proof
447	    of REF the dependency on
448	    \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) is reduced to its \(H^0\) functor and the short
449	    exact sequence of the closed point.
450	  \item The vanishing \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0\) for
451	    \(\deg(D) \geq 0\) on a genus-\(0\) curve is consumed as a named
452	    premise by REF; its closure is
453	    part of \texttt{RR.3} (where the cohomology of
454	    \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(d)\) is computed) and is recovered for
455	    a general smooth proper genus-\(0\) curve via the isomorphism
456	    \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) of \texttt{RR.4} or directly via the
457	    base-change argument inside \texttt{RR.3}.
458	  \item The classification ``a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
459	    curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g = 0\) is isomorphic to
460	    \(\mathbb P^1\)'' (Hartshorne Example~IV.1.3.5 proper) is the
461	    headline of \texttt{RR.4} (sibling chapter
462	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalIsoP1.tex}, to be added). The
463	    present chapter supplies one of its two inputs (the $\ell(D) =
464	    \deg(D) + 1\( identity); the other input is the degree-\)0$
465	    principal-divisor identity REF from
466	    \texttt{RR.1}.
467	  \item Higher-cohomology bookkeeping (\(\chi\) as an alternating sum
468	    over all \(i \geq 0\), the Grothendieck vanishing
469	    \(H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0\) for \(i > \dim C = 1\)) is not separately
470	    formalised here; the chapter consumes only the two-term form
471	    \(\chi(\mathcal F) = \dim H^0 - \dim H^1\) which is the standing
472	    curve specialisation.
473	\end{itemize}

11	/-!
12	# The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (RR.2_*)
13	
14	This file is the **RR.2_*** satellite sub-build chapter for the project's
15	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
17	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
18	
19	Together with `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`), `RR.2`
20	(`RRFormula.lean`), `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`), and `RR.4`
21	(`RationalCurveIso.lean`), the present chapter forms the four-stage
22	sub-build closing the project's headline **RR bridge**.
23	
24	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain consumed by `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` of
25	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` relies on the invertible-sheaf functor
26	`𝒪_C(-) : Div(C) → 𝐒𝐡(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)` sending a Weil divisor
27	`D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` to its associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` on `C`.
28	
29	This file hosts the four pinned declarations of the chapter
30	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` — the invertible sheaf
33	   `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve `C / k̄`,
34	   realised as Hartshorne's subsheaf of the function-field constant
35	   sheaf `K_C` (II §6 p. 144). Replaces the iter-174 typed-`sorry`
36	   placeholder currently living at `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean:168`.
37	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero` — the zero
38	   divisor `D = 0` gives the structure sheaf `𝒪_C(0) = 𝒪_C`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_singlePoint` — the
40	   closed-point specialisation `𝒪_C([P]) = lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
41	   of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
42	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add` — the
43	   Hartshorne IV.1.3 inductive-step short exact sequence
44	   `0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0` consumed by Lane H's
45	   `RRFormula` induction.
46	
47	## Status (iter-183 Lane K file-skeleton)
48	
49	This file is the **iter-183 Lane K** file-skeleton: each declaration
50	carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
51	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`).
52	All bodies are `sorry`; the iter-184+ closure follows the recipe in
53	chapter §"Sheaf-property correctness" (subsheaf-of-`K_C` per-open
54	constraint set, identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, sheaf property via
55	stalk-locality of the order conditions). The construction is
56	`noncomputable`.
57	
58	**3-tier disclosure** (per iter-181 vocabulary): each of the four
59	declarations is a **Tier-3 honest typed sorry** — the body is a
60	substantive mathematical construction (Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C`)
61	whose closure is iter-184+ work; the types encode genuine claims about
62	the invertible sheaf, its specialisations, and its SES additivity.
63	
64	## References
65	
66	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`.
67	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II §6 pp. 144–145 (definition of
68	`ℒ(D)`; Propositions 6.13, 6.15, 6.18; Remark 6.17.1) and IV §1 p. 296
69	(the `D ↝ D + [Y]` SES). Stacks Project tags 02RW (Weil divisors),
70	0AUW (sheaf `𝒪_X(D)`), 0BE3 (degree-zero of a principal divisor).
71	-/

80	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
81	
82	/-! ## §1. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor -/
83	
84	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
85	
86	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
87	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
88	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
90	
91	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth
92	proper curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144, definition of `ℒ(D)`).
93	
94	For a Weil divisor `D = Σ_Q n_Q · [Q] ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper
95	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the invertible sheaf
96	`𝒪_C(D) := ℒ(D)` is the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant
97	sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` (Hartshorne Proposition 6.15: on an integral scheme
98	the sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
99	field) defined section-wise on each open `U ⊆ C` as
100	`Γ(U, 𝒪_C(D)) = { f ∈ K(C) | f = 0 ∨ ord_Q(f) ≥ −n_Q ∀ prime divisor Q
101	∈ U }`, with restriction along `V ⊆ U` given by the identity on `K(C)`.
102	
103	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
104	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf carrier used
105	by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so that `H⁰` and
106	`H¹` of `𝒪_C(D)` are accessible via
107	`Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf D) 0/1`).
108	
109	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
110	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Sheaf-property
111	correctness"): per-open `Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order
112	conditions (the closure proofs reduce to `Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity
113	and the non-archimedean inequality of the DVR valuation at each prime
114	divisor); presheaf functor via the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; sheaf
115	property via gluing-by-stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at
116	each prime divisor). The construction parallels the project's existing
117	`Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf` / `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template in
118	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/`.
119	
120	**Coordination with `RRFormula.lean:168`.** The iter-174 typed-`sorry`
121	placeholder `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` in `RRFormula.lean` is
122	slated to be retired (Lane H) by re-export of this declaration. Both
123	files compile in isolation; cross-imports are coordinated by Lane H.
124	
125	Blueprint reference: `def:sheafOf` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144;
126	Propositions 6.13, 6.15; Remark 6.17.1).
127	
128	**iter-185 Lane K body fragment.** The case `D = 0` is implemented on the
129	nose as the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (Hartshorne's
130	``\(\mathcal O_C(0) = \mathcal O_C\)'' identification; chapter
131	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `lem:sheafOf_zero`). The general case
132	remains an honest typed `sorry` pending the iter-186+ Hartshorne
133	subsheaf-of-`K_C` construction described in the docstring above. The
134	`if`-branching uses `Classical.dec` (via `open Classical in`) on the
135	`WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)` carrier, which has no canonical
136	decidable equality. -/
137	noncomputable def sheafOf (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
138	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
139	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
140	  open Classical in
141	  if D = 0 then Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C else sorry
142	
143	/-! ## §2. Immediate corollaries -/
144	
145	/-- **Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf**
146	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_zero`).
147	
148	At `D = 0` the coefficient `n_Q = 0` at every prime divisor `Q`, so the
149	section condition of `sheafOf` reduces to the standard
150	"non-negative-order = regular" identification of the structure sheaf on
151	an integral scheme (Hartshorne II §6 immediately before Proposition 6.11).
152	Both sides are sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` with identity restriction
153	maps, so the equality of presheaves promotes to an equality of sheaves
154	of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules.
155	
156	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body closes
157	on iter-184+ closure of `sheafOf` together with the standard `Γ(U, 𝒪_C)`
158	identification.
159	
160	**iter-185 Lane K body**: closes via the explicit `D = 0` branch in the
161	`sheafOf` def above (modified iter-185 to land the structure-sheaf value
162	on the `0` divisor on the nose). Unfolding `sheafOf` exposes the
163	`if 0 = 0 then toModuleKSheaf C else sorry` and `if_pos rfl` picks the
164	true branch.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_zero`. -/
167	lemma sheafOf_zero :
168	    sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor) = Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C := by
169	  unfold sheafOf
170	  exact if_pos rfl
171	
172	/-- **Sheaf at a single closed point is the line bundle of `RR.3`**
173	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_singlePoint`).
174	
175	For a closed point `P ∈ C` viewed as a Weil divisor `[P] ∈ Div(C)` via
176	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint`, the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` of
177	`sheafOf` agrees on the nose with the closed-point line bundle
178	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P` of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
179	
180	The agreement is by unfolding both definitions: at `D = [P]`, the order
181	conditions of `sheafOf` (`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P`, `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`)
182	are exactly those characterising sections of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
183	(via `lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` of `OCofP.lean`).
184	
185	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Closes on
186	iter-184+ closure of both `sheafOf` and `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.
187	
188	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_singlePoint`. -/
189	lemma sheafOf_singlePoint (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
190	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
191	    sheafOf (C := C) (ofClosedPoint P hP) =
192	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh := by
193	  sorry
194	
195	/-- **Short exact sequence for `D ↝ D + [P]`** (Hartshorne IV.1.3
196	inductive step, p. 296; chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma
197	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`).
198	
199	For a Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` and a prime divisor `P` of `C`
200	(equivalently, a closed point on a smooth curve), the inclusion
201	`𝒪_C(D) ↪ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` of sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` fits into a short
202	exact sequence
203	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`
204	in `Sh(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)`, where `k(P)` is the skyscraper sheaf at `P` with
205	stalk `k̄`.
206	
207	The statement bundles the existence of a `ShortComplex` whose `X₁ ↦ X₂`
208	arm matches `sheafOf D ↪ sheafOf (Finsupp.single P 1 + D)` and that is
209	`ShortExact` (mono + epi + exact). The third term `X₃` is the
210	skyscraper `k(P)` — encoded here as a (nonempty) isomorphism class with
211	`Mathlib`'s `skyscraperSheaf` (decidability of `P.point ∈ U` supplied
212	classically). Lane H's `RRFormula` consumes this sequence via
213	χ-additivity to derive `χ(𝒪_C(D + [P])) = χ(𝒪_C(D)) + 1`.
214	
215	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
216	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Immediate
217	corollaries", Beat 1 + Beat 2): build the ideal-sheaf SES
218	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0`, tensor with `𝒪_C(D + [P])` (which is
219	locally free of rank 1, hence the tensor is exact), and identify the
220	three terms via `𝒪_C(−[P]) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D)` (Hartshorne 6.13(b)),
221	`𝒪_C ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` (tensor unit), and
222	`k(P) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ k(P)` (rank-1 stalk at `P`).
223	
224	The argument `D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than
225	`Scheme.WeilDivisor` so that the `Finsupp.single P 1 + D` term elaborates
226	cleanly (the addition is in the unambiguous Finsupp instance); the result
227	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
228	ℤ)`, matching the pattern of `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add`
229	in `RRFormula.lean`.
230	
231	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_ses_single_add` (Hartshorne IV.1, p. 296). -/
232	theorem sheafOf_ses_single_add
233	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor) :
234	    ∃ S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
235	        (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
236	          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)),
237	      S.ShortExact ∧
238	      S.X₁ = sheafOf (C := C) D ∧
239	      S.X₂ = sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single P 1 + D) ∧
240	      Nonempty (S.X₃ ≅ skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
241	        P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) := by
242	  sorry
243	
244	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
245	
246	end AlgebraicGeometry

131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
150	
151	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
152	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
153	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H⁰ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
154	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ⟶ F)` identifies the
155	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
156	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
157	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
158	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
159	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
160	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
161	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
162	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
163	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
164	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
165	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
166	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F)] :
167	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=
168	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k F X).symm

195	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is

197	integral $k$-curves: for each affine corner $X_i$ of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,

205	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
206	    [Module.Finite k
207	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
208	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶
209	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :

213	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
214	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
215	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
216	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
217	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object — see iter-014 abbrev). The class
218	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
219	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
220	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
221	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
222	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
223	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
224	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
225	    Prop where
226	  subsingleton_HModule' : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
227	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U → ∀ i, 0 < i →
228	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
229	
230	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
231	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
232	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
233	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
234	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M → Module.Finite R M` instance (any
235	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
236	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
237	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
238	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
239	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
240	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
241	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
242	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
243	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
244	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
245	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
246	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
247	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
248	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
249	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
250	  inferInstance

252	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
253	algebraic statement that the global Hom group
254	`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
255	`Γ(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
256	proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
257	$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
258	connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
259	geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).
260	
261	**Historical note on the abandoned per-affine-open variant.** An earlier
262	iter-041 attempt packaged Hom-finiteness of
263	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)` **for every
264	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
265	Hom group is `≃ₗ[k] Γ(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
266	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
267	affine open — e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
268	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
269	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
270	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
271	scaffolding.
272	
273	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
274	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
275	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
276	vanishing). -/
277	class IsHModuleHomFinite
278	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
279	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
280	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
281	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
282	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
283	
284	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness class
285	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
286	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H⁰ algebraic bridge
287	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ⟶ F)`)
288	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
289	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
290	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
291	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
292	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
293	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
294	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
295	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
296	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F

298	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
299	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
300	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
301	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
302	patterns. -/
303	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
305	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
306	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
307	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
308	
309	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰
310	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
311	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
312	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
313	
314	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
315	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
316	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
317	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
318	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
319	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
320	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
321	
322	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
323	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (where the algebra
324	structure on `Γ(C, ⊤)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
325	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
326	transport the base ring from intermediate `Γ(Spec k, ⊤)` to `k` along
327	the ring iso `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
328	of algebra maps reduces to showing
329	`kToSection C (op ⊤).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
330	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `⊤ ⟶ ⊤` hom-set +
331	`Functor.map_id`.
332	
333	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
334	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
335	`constantSheafΓAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
336	`Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
337	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
338	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
339	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
340	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
341	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
342	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
343	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
344	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
345	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
346	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
347	  have hM_inter :
348	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
349	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by

450	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
451	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
452	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
453	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
454	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
455	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
456	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
457	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
458	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
459	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
460	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
461	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
462	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
463	
464	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
465	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
466	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
467	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
468	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
469	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
470	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
471	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
472	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
473	
474	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
475	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
476	
477	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
478	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
479	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
480	      as `k`-modules.
481	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
482	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
483	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
484	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
485	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
486	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
487	
488	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
489	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
490	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
491	
492	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
493	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
494	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
495	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
496	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
497	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
498	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
499	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
500	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
501	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
502	  module_finite_hom := by
503	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
504	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
505	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
506	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
507	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
508	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
509	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
510	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
513	
514	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
515	
516	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
517	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
518	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
519	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
520	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
521	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
522	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
523	
524	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
525	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
526	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
527	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
528	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
529	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
531	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
532	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
533	
534	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
535	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
536	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
537	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
538	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
539	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
540	acyclic. -/
541	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
542	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
543	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
544	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
545	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
546	
547	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
548	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
549	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Presheaf
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
60

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)–(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7–8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u₂ v₂
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
36	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
43	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
51	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
57	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
63	
64	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
65	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
66	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
67	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
68	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
69	
70	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
71	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
72	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
73	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
74	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
75	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
76	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
77	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
78	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
79	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
80	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
81	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
82	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }
83	
84	end Adjunction
85	
86	end CategoryTheory
87	
88	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
89	
90	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
91	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
92	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
93	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
94	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
95	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
96	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
97	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
98	  inferInstance
99	
100	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
101	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
102	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
103	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
104	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
105	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
106	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
107	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
108	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
109	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
110	
111	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
112	
113	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
114	
115	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
116	
117	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
118	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
119	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
120	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
121	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
122	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
123	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
124	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
125	
126	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
127	map of `kToSection`. -/
128	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
129	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
130	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
131	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
132	
133	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
134	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
140	natural in `U`. -/
141	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
142	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
143	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
144	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
145	  rfl
146	
147	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
148	`algebraMap`. -/
149	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
150	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
151	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
152	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
153	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
154	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
155	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
156	
157	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
158	
159	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
160	
161	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
162	
163	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
164	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
165	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
166	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
167	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
168	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
169	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
170	      map_add' := fun x y => by
171	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
172	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
173	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
174	        congr 1
175	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
176	          (C := C) f r }
177	  map_id U := by
178	    ext x
179	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
180	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
181	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
182	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
183	    ext x
184	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
185	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
186	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
187	
188	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
189	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
190	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
191	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
192	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
193	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
194	  rfl
195	
196	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.SheafProperty
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u v
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
43	
44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀

input.new_string:
private theorem Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) = 1 := by
  -- Step 1: H⁰-bridge LinearEquiv chain (existing project infrastructure):
  --   HModule kbar (toModuleKSheaf C) 0
  --     ≃ₗ ((constantSheaf).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)   [HModule_zero_linearEquiv]
  --     ≃ₗ (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))           [constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv]
  --     ≃ₗ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)                                       [homFromOne_linearEquiv]
  --     ≃ₗ (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)                                    [SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top]
  -- which on the underlying type is `C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤)`.
  let LE1 :=
    Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
  let LE2 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
  let LE3 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
  let LE4 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar
      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
  let LE := ((LE1.trans LE2).trans LE3).trans LE4
  rw [LE.finrank_eq]
  -- Step 2: `(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` is definitionally
  -- `ModuleCat.of kbar (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (per `toModuleKPresheaf_obj`),
  -- so we transport to the global-sections ring and apply
  -- `Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap` + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`.
  haveI hFin : Module.Finite kbar
      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper kbar C
  haveI hNE : Nonempty
      (↑(⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) := by
    haveI : Nonempty C.left := AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.nonempty (X := C.left)
    obtain ⟨x⟩ := ‹Nonempty C.left›
    exact ⟨⟨x, trivial⟩⟩
  haveI hDom : IsDomain
      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.component_integral (X := C.left) ⊤
  exact Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap
    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := kbar)
      (K := C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))))

          : Set C.left.toTopCat) := by

    exact ⟨⟨x, trivial⟩⟩

      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.component_integral (X := C.left) ⊤
  exact Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap
    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := kbar)

input.new_string:
-- Convert the ModuleCat coercion to the underlying global sections ring.
  change Module.finrank kbar
      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) = 1
  -- Module-finiteness via the iter-044 Stein input.
  haveI hFin : Module.Finite kbar
      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper kbar C
  -- Integrality of `C.left` propagates to the global sections being a domain
  -- (`IsIntegral.component_integral`), with the nonempty side-condition
  -- discharged from `IsIntegral.nonempty`.
  haveI hNECurve : Nonempty C.left.toTopCat :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.nonempty
  haveI hNE : Nonempty
      ↥((⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Set C.left.toTopCat) :=
    ⟨⟨hNECurve.some, trivial⟩⟩
  haveI hDom : IsDomain
      (C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.component_integral
      (X := C.left) (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
  -- `Module.Finite kbar A` → `Algebra.IsIntegral kbar A` (auto-instance).
  -- Then `IsAlgClosed` + integrality + domain ⇒ algebraMap is bijective ⇒ finrank = 1.
  exact Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap
    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := kbar)
      (K := C.left.presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))))
