80	\section{The morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from two global sections}
81	\begin{lemma}
82	\leanok
83	[Morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from a globally generating pair of sections]
84	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
85	  
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme over a field \(\bar k\), let \(\mathcal L\) be an invertible
88	  sheaf on \(X\), and let \(s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)\) be two global
89	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
90	  \[
91	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
92	  \]
93	  (Equivalently, \(s_0\) and \(s_1\) generate \(\mathcal L\) at every point.) Then
94	  there exists a unique morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
95	  \[
96	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
97	  \]
98	  whose pullback of \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)\) is \(\mathcal L\) and whose
99	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
100	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))\( are \)s_0\( and \)s_1$ respectively. On the
101	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
102	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\( the morphism \)\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}\( is the map ``\)x
103	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)\(''; on the chart \)D_+(x_1)\( it is the map ``\)x
104	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
105	  \(D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}\) by the cocycle identity
106	  \((s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1\).
107	\end{lemma}
108	\begin{proof}
109	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
110	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to \(\mathbb P^n\)''), specialised to
111	  \(n = 1\). Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
112	  under the name
113	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
114	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
115	  \begin{quote}
116	  \small
117	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{\(\sigma\) A\} [CommRing A]
118	    [SetLike \(\sigma\) A] [AddSubgroupClass \(\sigma\) A] (\(\mathcal A\) :
119	    \(\mathbb N\) → \(\sigma\)) [GradedRing \(\mathcal A\)] \{X : Scheme\}\\
120	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj \(\top\))\\
121	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant \(\mathcal A\)).toIdeal = \(\top\)) :\\
122	    \phantom{}\quad(X \(\longrightarrow\) Proj \(\mathcal A\))}
123	  \end{quote}
124	  Specialising to \(\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) with the standard $\mathbb
125	  N\(-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded \)\bar k$-algebra
126	  homomorphism \(f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) and a
127	  proof that the irrelevant ideal \((x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) maps to
128	  the unit ideal of \(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
129	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
130	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of \)\mathbb
131	  P^n\( as a \)\mathrm{Proj}\(). When \)\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
132	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
133	  cover \(X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)\), where on each chart the sections become
134	  honest global functions (\(s_1 / s_0\) on \(D(s_0)\), \(s_0 / s_1\) on \(D(s_1)\),
135	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
136	  project-bespoke wrapper
137	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
138	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
139	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
140	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
141	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
142	  a separated scheme: two morphisms \(\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1\)
143	  with \(\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i\) for \(i = 0, 1\) agree on the basic-open
144	  charts \(D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)\) (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
145	  the same rational function), and these charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\), so the
146	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
147	\end{proof}
148	\section{Degree of a morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via its pole divisor}
149	% NOTE (iter-182 plan-phase Pin 2 signature strengthening). The iter-177
150	% file-skeleton placeholder existential
151	%   "$\exists (d : \mathbb N) (D : C.\mathrm{WeilDivisor}),\
152	%      0 < d \wedge D.\mathrm{degree} = d$"
153	% was vacuous in \(\varphi\) (no constraint tying \(D\) to \(\varphi\) or the
154	% point \(\infty\)). The iter-182 plan-phase refactor (refactor slug
155	% `pin2-sig-strengthen`) replaces it with the substantive existential
156	% pinning \(D\) to \(\varphi^*[\infty]\) and the degree to the function-field
157	% extension degree \([K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\). The new typed-sorry def
158	% `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` (in
159	% `RationalCurveIso.lean`, analogous to the iter-181 typed-sorry def
160	% `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` of OCofP.lean) carries the
161	% Weil divisor \(\varphi^*[\infty]\); its body is queued for iter-183+
162	% via the affine-chart `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` route.
163	\begin{lemma}
164	\leanok
165	[Degree of a non-constant morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via the preimage of \(\infty\)]
166	  \label{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor}
167	  
168	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor}
169	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S2, opening paragraph of "Hurwitz's Theorem"
170	  % (definition of the degree of a finite morphism of curves) (read from
171	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
172	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
173	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
174	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
175	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.2, p.~299, opening of \S2.}
176	  Let \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a non-constant morphism
177	  from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\). Then
178	  \(\varphi\) is finite, its function-field extension
179	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\) (the canonical
180	  \(\varphi\)-induced map, supplied as an
181	  \texttt{[Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField C.functionField]}
182	  instance argument on the Lean side) is finite, and there exists a Weil
183	  divisor \(D \in \Div(C)\) such that
184	  \[
185	    D \;=\; \varphi^*[\infty]
186	    \quad\text{and}\quad
187	    \deg(D) \;=\; [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,].
188	  \]
189	  Equivalently, the morphism \(\varphi\) satisfies
190	  \[
191	    [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,] \;=\;
192	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
193	                  \ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [P]\,\right),
194	  \]
195	  where \(t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)\) is a local parameter at \(\infty\)
196	  (equivalently \(t_\infty = 1/u\) for the standard affine coordinate \(u\) on
197	  \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\)); and for any closed point
198	  \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) the same identification gives
199	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\).
200	  \emph{Convention on the pole divisor.} The Weil divisor
201	  \(\varphi^*[\infty]\) is exposed in Lean as
202	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \(\varphi\)}, a
203	  noncomputable definition introduced (as a typed \texttt{sorry}) at the
204	  iter-182 plan-phase signature strengthening of this lemma. The body of
205	  \texttt{poleDivisor} is queued for iter-183+ closure via the standard
206	  affine-chart construction (\texttt{Ideal.sum\_ramification\_inertia}
207	  in Mathlib, applied at each closed point \(P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)\)
208	  to read off the local ramification multiplicity
209	  \(\ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty)\) from the maximal ideal at \(\infty\) in
210	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)). On the Lean side, the existential output of
211	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} packages
212	  \(D = \texttt{Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor}\;\varphi\) together with
213	  \(\deg(D) = \operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(\mathbb P^1)} K(C)\),
214	  mirroring the OCofP pattern in which a typed-sorry def
215	  (\texttt{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}) is introduced
216	  upstream of its consumer lemma.
217	\end{lemma}
218	\begin{proof}
219	  A non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves over \(\bar k\) is
220	  automatically finite. Indeed, the image of \(\varphi\) is a closed
221	  irreducible subset of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) that is not a point (else
222	  \(\varphi\) would be constant) and not empty, so it is all of $\mathbb
223	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (the only proper irreducible closed subset of \)\mathbb
224	  P^1_{\bar k}\( is a point); hence \)\varphi$ is surjective. A surjective
225	  morphism between smooth proper irreducible curves of equal dimension is
226	  finite (proper + quasi-finite, where quasi-finiteness comes from the
227	  fact that \(\varphi\) has zero-dimensional fibres: a positive-dimensional
228	  fibre would force a one-dimensional irreducible component in the fibre,
229	  contradicting irreducibility of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) and \(C\) at
230	  generic points). Hence the function-field extension
231	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) = \varphi^\# K(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\)
232	  is finite.
233	  The identification \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\) for
234	  any closed point \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) is the multiplicativity of the
235	  degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9): for a finite morphism
236	  \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) of smooth proper curves and any divisor $D \in
237	  \Div(C')\(, \)\deg(\varphi^* D) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg(D)$. Specialising
238	  to \(D = [Q]\) (a divisor of degree \(1\) since \(\kappa(Q) = \bar k\)) gives
239	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = \deg(\varphi)\). Equivalently, the degree of
240	  \(\varphi\) equals the number of preimages of any closed point of $\mathbb
241	  P^1\(, counted with the ramification multiplicities \)\ord_P(\varphi^*
242	  t_\infty)\( at the preimage points \)P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$.
243	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The lemma threads two auxiliary inputs:
244	  ``non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is finite'' (the
245	  surjective-and-proper-and-quasi-finite argument above; Hartshorne~II.6.8)
246	  and ``multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback'' (Hartshorne~II.6.9
247	  and the multiplicativity of \(\deg\) under composition of \(\div\) and
248	  \(\deg\); the closely related ``\(\mathrm{div}(f)\) has degree zero'' specialisation
249	  is \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}). Both are separable
250	  sub-lemmas inside \texttt{RR.4}'s Lean file
251	  (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) and are
252	  slotted for closure in a follow-up iteration after the
253	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} skeleton lands.
254	\end{proof}
255	\section{A degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism}
256	% NOTE (iter-181 Lane I signature refinement; see analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md
257	% Decision 2, verdict DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). The iter-177 file-skeleton
258	% placeholder hypothesis "Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)"
259	% (an abstract function-field iso, unrelated to \(\varphi\)) was strictly
260	% weaker than the birational-extension body needs and was refined in
261	% iter-181 to the explicit function-field-extension-degree formulation
262	% [K(C):K(C')] = 1, i.e. Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
263	% = 1, with C.functionField a C'.functionField-algebra via the canonical
264	% \varphi-induced map (Scheme.Hom.stalkMap at the generic point composed
265	% with IsFractionRing.lift). The proof-prose body below (the Hartshorne
266	% I.6.12 chain + scheme-theoretic alternative argument) is unchanged ---
267	% only the input shape changes.
268	\begin{lemma}
269	\leanok
270	[Degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism]
271	  \label{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso}
272	  
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one}
274	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field-to-curve
275	  % equivalence of categories) (read from
276	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 62, doc p.~45)
277	  % + [Hartshorne], IV \S2 opening paragraph (degree of a finite morphism
278	  % of curves is the function-field extension degree) (read from
279	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
280	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.12. \emph{The following three categories are
281	  % equivalent:}
282	  % (i) \emph{nonsingular projective curves, and dominant morphisms;}
283	  % (ii) \emph{quasi-projective curves, and dominant rational maps;}
284	  % (iii) \emph{function fields of dimension 1 over \(k\), and \(k\)-homomorphisms.}"
285	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
286	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
287	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
288	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, I.6 Corollary~6.12 (p.~45) + IV.2 opening
289	  (p.~299).}
290	  Let \(C, C'\) be smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves over an
291	  algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) be
292	  a non-constant morphism whose induced function-field extension is of
293	  degree one,
294	  \[
295	    [\,K(C) : K(C')\,] \;=\; 1,
296	  \]
297	  formally
298	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(C')} K(C) = 1\) in the Lean signature.
299	  Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
300	  \emph{Convention on the function-field map.} At each call-site of this
301	  lemma, the \(K(C')\)-algebra structure on \(K(C)\) used to interpret the
302	  degree \([K(C) : K(C')]\) is the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
303	  function-field map, i.e.\ the composite
304	  \[
305	    K(C') \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{germ}\;}\;
306	    \mathcal O_{C', \varphi(\xi_C)}
307	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\varphi^\#\;}\;
308	    \mathcal O_{C, \xi_C}
309	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{frac.}\,\text{lift}\;}\; K(C)
310	  \]
311	  of the germ at the image of the generic point \(\xi_C \in C\) with the
312	  stalk-map of \(\varphi\) at \(\xi_C\) and the fraction-field lift at
313	  \(\xi_C\) (per \texttt{analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md}, Decision~2). On
314	  the Lean side this canonical instance is carried as a
315	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
316	  to \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one}; consumers
317	  supply it explicitly at each call-site.
318	\end{lemma}
319	\begin{proof}
320	  The function-field hypothesis \([K(C) : K(C')] = 1\) states that the
321	  canonical \(\varphi\)-induced map \(\varphi^\# \colon K(C') \to K(C)\) is a
322	  finite extension of degree one; since \(K(C')\) is a field and the
323	  extension has dimension one as a \(K(C')\)-vector space, \(\varphi^\#\) is
324	  an isomorphism of function fields \(K(C') \cong K(C)\). (Equivalently, by
325	  the IV.2-opening identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C):K(C')]\), this is
326	  the statement \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\); the morphism \(\varphi\) is finite in
327	  this case by the standard argument that a non-constant morphism of
328	  smooth proper curves is finite, identical to the
329	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} preamble.)
330	  By Hartshorne's Corollary~I.6.12, the assignment \(C \mapsto \bar k(C)\) is
331	  an equivalence of categories between smooth projective curves over $\bar
332	  k$ (with dominant morphisms) and function fields of transcendence degree
333	  one over \(\bar k\) (with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms). Under this equivalence,
334	  the isomorphism \(\bar k(C') \cong \bar k(C)\) produced by the previous
335	  paragraph lifts to a unique isomorphism of smooth proper curves $C \to
336	  C'\(, and this isomorphism agrees with \)\varphi$ on the generic point (by
337	  construction: it is the inverse-image of \(\varphi^\#\) under the
338	  equivalence). The two morphisms \(C \to C'\) that agree on the generic
339	  point of the integral scheme \(C\) agree everywhere (a morphism out of an
340	  integral scheme to a separated scheme is determined by its restriction
341	  to the generic point, Hartshorne~II Ex.~4.2). Hence \(\varphi\) is itself
342	  the lift, and is therefore an isomorphism.
343	  \emph{Alternative direct argument.} Without invoking the
344	  equivalence-of-categories framing, the same conclusion is obtained
345	  scheme-theoretically: a finite degree-\(1\) morphism $\varphi \colon C \to
346	  C'$ between integral normal noetherian schemes is an isomorphism.
347	  Indeed, the direct image \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C\) is a coherent
348	  \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module that is generically of rank $\deg(\varphi) =
349	  1\(, and an inclusion \)\mathcal O_{C'} \hookrightarrow \varphi_*
350	  \mathcal O_C\( of coherent rank-\)1$ sheaves whose generic ranks agree
351	  is an isomorphism over the locus where the quotient
352	  \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C / \mathcal O_{C'}\) has trivial stalk. The
353	  quotient is a torsion coherent \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module of generic rank
354	  \(\deg(\varphi) - 1 = 0\), hence is itself torsion-free (a
355	  finitely-generated torsion module over a Dedekind domain of generic
356	  rank zero is zero), so vanishes. Therefore $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C =
357	  \mathcal O_{C'}\( and \)\varphi$ is the structure morphism of an
358	  isomorphism. Cf.\ Stacks tag~\textbf{0AVX} for the categorical version.
359	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The Lean signature
360	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one} consumes a
361	  non-constant morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) together with a
362	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
363	  (the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced function-field map) and the
364	  function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis
365	  \texttt{Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1}, and
366	  produces an isomorphism \(C \cong C'\) in the slice category
367	  \(\mathtt{Over (Spec (.of\ kbar))}\). The proof body threads the Mathlib
368	  API \texttt{IsIntegralHom.iso\_of\_isUnit} (or the equivalent
369	  \texttt{IsAffineHom + Finite + RankOne} chain) and, where needed, the
370	  project-bespoke ``finite-morphism-of-degree-one is an isomorphism''
371	  helper. The Hartshorne~I.6.12 equivalence-of-categories framing is the
372	  intended high-level proof structure; the scheme-theoretic argument
373	  above is the spelled-out form for the Lean body.
374	\end{proof}
375	\section{Headline: genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)}
376	\begin{theorem}
377	
378	\leanok
379	[Genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)]
380	  \label{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}
381	  
382	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
383	  % already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely
384	  % suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two distinct
385	  % points of \(X\) and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor \(D = P - Q\).
386	  % Since \(\deg(K - D) = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have \(l(K - D) = 0\),
387	  % and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of degree 0, so by
388	  % (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words \(P \sim Q\). But this implies
389	  % \(X\) is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
390	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
391	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, and let \(C\) be a smooth
392	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\).
393	  Then there exists an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
394	  \[
395	    \varphi \colon C \stackrel{\sim}{\longrightarrow} \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}.
396	  \]
397	  Concretely, the Lean signature
398	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} (the existing pinned
399	  target at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290}) is
400	  closed by the proof below; the existing interface block
401	  \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
402	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr} is its consumer-side restatement,
403	  pinning the same Lean declaration.
404	\end{theorem}
405	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the
406	% statement block above (Hartshorne states the classification and its
407	% proof inside the single Example; the proof is the second half beginning
408	% "We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) \ldots"). The present
409	% proof body restates Hartshorne's argument in the project's notation
410	% (linear-system construction \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C
411	% ([P]))\(, degree-\)1$ identification, isomorphism).
412	\begin{proof}
413	  Over the algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) the smooth proper
414	  geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) has closed points, all of which are
415	  \(\bar k\)-rational (the residue field of a closed point of a curve over
416	  \(\bar k\) is a finite extension of \(\bar k\), hence is \(\bar k\) itself by
417	  algebraic closedness). Fix one such \(\bar k\)-rational point \(P \in C\);
418	  this is the marked point used to build the linear system below.
419	  \emph{Step 1: non-constant function \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\).}
420	  By Hartshorne's Riemann--Roch in genus zero
421	  (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}) applied to the
422	  effective divisor \([P]\) of degree \(1\), we obtain
423	  \(\ell([P]) = \deg([P]) + 1 = 2\). The constant section
424	  \(1 \in \bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) accounts for a
425	  one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-subspace; the two-dimensional quotient supplies
426	  a non-constant section $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
427	  \bar k \cdot 1$. This is the corollary
428	  \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
429	  chapter, \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OCofP.tex}).
430	  \emph{Step 2: morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via the linear system \((1, f)\).}
431	  The pair \((1, f) \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))^2\) generates the
432	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) at every point of \(C\): at points
433	  \(Q \in C \setminus \{P\}\) the section \(1\) does not vanish in
434	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])_Q \cong \mathcal O_{C, Q}\), and at the marked point
435	  \(P\) the section \(f\) does not vanish in \(\mathcal O_C([P])_P\) (because \(f\)
436	  has a pole of order exactly \(1\) at \(P\) in the rational-function
437	  picture: \(f \in K(C) \setminus \bar k\) with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = [P]\)).
438	  Therefore by \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections} there
439	  exists a unique morphism
440	  \[
441	    \varphi \;:=\; \varphi_{(1, f)} \colon C \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
442	  \]
443	  with \(\varphi^* x_0 = 1\) and \(\varphi^* x_1 = f\) (so the pullback of
444	  the standard ratio coordinate \(u = x_1 / x_0\) on $\mathbb P^1 \setminus
445	  \{\infty\}\( is \)f$, and the pullback of the inverse coordinate
446	  \(t_\infty = x_0 / x_1 = 1 / f\) is the local parameter \(1/f\) at \(P\)).
447	  \emph{Step 3: \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\) via the pole divisor of \(f\).} The
448	  morphism \(\varphi\) is non-constant (since \(f\) is non-constant as a
449	  rational function on \(C\), the pullback \(\varphi^* u = f\) takes
450	  infinitely many values, ruling out a constant \(\varphi\)). By
451	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} applied to \(\varphi\),
452	  \[
453	    \deg(\varphi)
454	    \;=\;
455	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{Q \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
456	                  \ord_Q(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [Q]\,\right).
457	  \]
458	  We compute the pole divisor of \(f\) as a rational function on \(C\):
459	  since \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) with \(f\) non-constant and
460	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) the line bundle of the effective divisor \([P]\), the
461	  rational-function \(f\) has pole divisor \((f)_\infty = [P]\) (the
462	  unique pole of order \(1\) at \(P\), no other poles; this is the standard
463	  identification of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\) with the rational functions
464	  \(g \in K(C)\) satisfying \(\mathrm{div}(g) + D \geq 0\), specialised to
465	  \(D = [P]\)). Hence \(\varphi^{-1}(\infty) = \{P\}\) with multiplicity~\(1\)
466	  and
467	  \[
468	    \deg(\varphi) \;=\; \deg([P]) \;=\; 1.
469	  \]
470	  \emph{Step 4: \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.} By Hartshorne's IV.2
471	  identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\), the
472	  degree-one conclusion of Step~3 rewrites as
473	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C) = 1\) (with
474	  the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
475	  \texttt{[Algebra \,(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField\,
476	   C.functionField]} instance). By
477	  \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} applied to the non-constant
478	  morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) (smooth proper
479	  geometrically irreducible curves over \(\bar k\) on both sides), under
480	  this function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis, \(\varphi\) is an
481	  isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
482	  This produces the required isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)
483	  closing the headline target
484	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}.
485	  \emph{Comparison with Hartshorne's original argument.} Hartshorne's
486	  Example~IV.1.3.5 phrases the argument in terms of two distinct points
487	  \(P, Q \in C\) and the degree-zero divisor \(D = P - Q\), deriving \(P \sim Q\)
488	  and concluding via II.6.10.1 that \(C\) is rational; combined with I.6.12
489	  this yields \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\). The present proof restates the same
490	  combinatorial content via the more directly formalisable linear-system
491	  construction: instead of going through the \(P \sim Q\) statement and
492	  invoking II.6.10.1 as a black box, we produce the explicit morphism
493	  \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1\) from the two sections \((1, f)\) of
494	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) given by Riemann--Roch and identify its degree
495	  directly. The two arguments are mathematically equivalent and have the
496	  same dependency footprint (Riemann--Roch in genus zero \(\Rightarrow\)
497	  \(\ell([P]) = 2\) \(\Rightarrow\) non-constant \(f\) \(\Rightarrow\) degree-\(1\)
498	  map to \(\mathbb P^1\) \(\Rightarrow\) iso); the linear-system phrasing maps
499	  more cleanly onto the Mathlib \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} API and
500	  the project's existing Weil-divisor/degree pipeline of \texttt{RR.1}.
501	\end{proof}
502	\begin{remark}
503	[Drop of \texttt{sorryAx} on the genus-\(0\) Albanese keystone]
504	  \label{rmk:rr4_unblocks_sorryAx_on_rigidity_genus0}
505	  Closure of \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} retires the
506	  \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
507	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}
508	  (\texttt{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of
509	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr}, body landed iter-166), whose
510	  \emph{first} of two upstream \texttt{sorry} sources is the
511	  \texttt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} target this chapter closes. The
512	  \emph{second} upstream is the helper
513	  \texttt{morphism\_P1\_to\_grpScheme\_const}'s residual sorries, tracked
514	  separately under the genus-\(0\) Albanese arm of \texttt{Jacobian.tex}.
515	\end{remark}
516	\section{Out of scope}
517	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4} and
518	are sequenced elsewhere:
519	\begin{itemize}
520	  \item The general ``smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) of
521	    genus \(0\) over a \emph{non-closed} field \(k\) with a \(k\)-rational point
522	    \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' statement is the \(\bar k \to k\) descent of
523	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}. The descent is hosted in
524	    \texttt{Jacobian.tex} via \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key}; it consumes
525	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} together with Galois descent for
526	    smooth proper curves and is out of scope for \texttt{RR.4}.
527	  \item The Mathlib API \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}
528	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}) is
529	    consumed as a black box by
530	    \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}; its own proof inside
531	    Mathlib (the universal property of \(\mathrm{Proj}\), graded-ring
532	    presentation of \(\mathbb P^n\)) is not re-derived here.
533	  \item Multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9)
534	    is consumed as a black box by \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor};
535	    its proof is a separable sub-lemma threaded inside
536	    \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} and is
537	    \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4}'s blueprint statement (it lives at
538	    the same logical level as the closely related
539	    \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}).
540	  \item The full equivalence of categories ``smooth projective curves over
541	    \(\bar k\) with dominant morphisms'' \(\cong\) ``function fields of
542	    transcendence degree~\(1\) over \(\bar k\) with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms''
543	    (Hartshorne~I.6.12) is consumed as a high-level framing in
544	    \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} but is \emph{not} re-formalised at
545	    the equivalence-of-categories level here. The proof body in the Lean
546	    file threads the spelled-out scheme-theoretic ``finite-degree-one
547	    morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism''
548	    argument (Stacks~\textbf{0AVX}), which is the minimal-Mathlib-dependence
549	    pin compatible with the chapter's downstream needs.
550	  \item ``A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\)
551	    has at least two distinct \(\bar k\)-points'' (the non-empty / infinite
552	    cardinality of \(C(\bar k)\)) is consumed implicitly by the present
553	    chapter but its formal statement lives in the project's curve API
554	    (\texttt{Genus.tex} / \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Genus}); the chapter
555	    does not restate it.
556	  \item Cohomology of the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a curve
557	    (Hartshorne~III.5 + IV.1) is fully consumed via \texttt{RR.3}; in
558	    particular the \(H^1\)-vanishing of \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a genus-\(0\)
559	    curve is part of \texttt{RR.3}'s payload feeding
560	    \texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}, and is not
561	    re-derived here.
562	\end{itemize}

1	# Analogy: Pin 2 — `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne II.6.9 / IV §2)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ratcurveiso-pins (Pin 2)
8	
9	## Iteration
10	181
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	Does Mathlib have either of (a) `Scheme.Hom.degree` for a morphism between
15	smooth proper curves over a field, (b) the pullback of a Weil divisor
16	`f^*D` on a scheme, (c) the Hartshorne II.6.9 identity
17	`deg(f^*D) = deg(f) · deg(D)`? If yes, the project should align with the
18	Mathlib idiom for
19	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`
20	(`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:296-306`).
21	
22	## Project artifact(s)
23	
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:296-306` —
25	  `theorem morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` with body `sorry`. The
26	  statement asserts `∃ (d : ℕ) (D : C.left.WeilDivisor), 0 < d ∧
27	  Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D = (d : ℤ)` for a non-constant
28	  `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄`.
29	- `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:92-298` — the
30	  project-bespoke `Scheme.PrimeDivisor`, `Scheme.WeilDivisor`,
31	  `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree`, `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` (all
32	  `sorry`-bodied skeletons).
33	
34	## Decisions identified
35	
36	Three coupled decisions:
37	
38	1. Does Mathlib already define the *degree of a morphism* between
39	   schemes (or specifically between integral / smooth-proper-curve
40	   schemes)?
41	2. Does Mathlib define the *Weil-divisor pullback* `f^* : Div(Y) → Div(X)`
42	   for a (suitable) morphism of schemes?
43	3. Does Mathlib offer the multiplicativity-of-degree formula
44	   `deg(f^*D) = deg(f) · deg(D)`?
45	
46	### Decision 1: degree of a morphism between schemes
47	
48	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE. Search confirms Mathlib has no
49	  `Scheme.Hom.degree`, no `Morphism.degree`, no
50	  `AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField.transcendenceDegree`. The closest is:
51	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField` —
52	    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField (X : Scheme) [IrreducibleSpace X] : CommRingCat`
53	    (the field at the generic point, abbreviation `K(X)`).
54	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField:instIsFractionRing...` —
55	    when `X` is integral, `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk x) X.functionField`.
56	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Finite` — `IsFinite f` /
57	    `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`.
58	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap` — `Scheme.RationalMap`,
59	    `ofFunctionField` / `fromFunctionField`.
60	  - `Mathlib.FieldTheory.SeparableDegree.Field.finSepDegree`,
61	    `Mathlib.FieldTheory.IntermediateField.Adjoin.Basic.IntermediateField.adjoin.finrank`,
62	    `Module.finrank` over a field extension.
63	
64	  So the **field-theoretic ingredient** for "degree of a finite morphism
65	  of integral schemes" — namely `[K(C) : K(C')]` via
66	  `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField` — is present, but the
67	  *bridge* (i.e. wrap "finrank of the function-field extension" as a
68	  named `Scheme.Hom.degree` with the multiplicativity API) is absent.
69	
70	- **Project's current path**: define the degree implicitly inside the
71	  existence statement `∃ d, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = (d : ℤ)`. The `d` here is
72	  conceptually `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]` but the lemma never names a separate
73	  `degree_of_morphism` definition.
74	
75	- **Gap**: divergent-and-isolated. Mathlib has neither a `degree` API
76	  for `Scheme.Hom` nor a Weil-divisor type for schemes — the project
77	  could not "align" even if it tried. The current statement (existence
78	  of a positive-degree divisor) is the right substantive content given
79	  the project-bespoke `Scheme.WeilDivisor` type.
80	
81	- **Cost of divergence**: zero, because no Mathlib API exists to
82	  fragment with. The eventual *body* of the lemma will route through
83	  `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField` + the
84	  project's own `WeilDivisor.pullback` (to be built) + a coordinate-by-
85	  coordinate identification.
86	
87	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. Mathlib has no scheme-level morphism-degree
88	  API to align with; the project's statement is the correct shape.
89	
90	### Decision 2: Weil-divisor pullback `f^* : Div(Y) → Div(X)`
91	
92	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE for schemes. The only "divisor pullback" in
93	  Mathlib is for the complex-analytic case:
94	  - `Mathlib.Analysis.Meromorphic.Divisor.MeromorphicOn.divisor` (complex
95	    analytic; takes a meromorphic function on `U ⊆ 𝕜` and produces a
96	    divisor).
97	
98	  For affine commutative rings Mathlib has:
99	  - `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup.CommRing.Pic` (the Picard group as an
100	    isomorphism class of invertible modules) — but this is for a single
101	    commutative ring, not for a scheme, and it does not expose a
102	    "divisor of a function" or pullback API.
103	
104	  The most directly relevant Mathlib infrastructure for the eventual
105	  body is:
106	  - `Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic.Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
107	    (for a finite extension of Dedekind domains, the sum
108	    `Σ_{P over p} e(P|p) · f(P|p) = [L:K]`). This is the algebraic
109	    incarnation of Hartshorne II.6.9 specialised to Dedekind bases.
110	  - `Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic.Ideal.finrank_quotient_map`
111	    (the closely related `finrank` identity).
112	
113	- **Project's current path**: `Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback` does NOT
114	  exist in `WeilDivisor.lean` yet; the iter-178+ body of Pin 2 is
115	  expected to introduce a project-private pullback construction (per
116	  the lemma's docstring comment at L283-292).
117	
118	- **Gap**: divergent-and-isolated. Same reason as Decision 1: Mathlib
119	  has no scheme-level WeilDivisor to pull back.
120	
121	- **Cost of divergence**: zero now; non-zero once the project starts
122	  building. The natural body uses `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` on
123	  each affine chart (pick an affine open `Spec A ⊂ C'` covering `∞`,
124	  then the preimage `Spec B ⊂ C` is finite over `Spec A` with
125	  `A → B` Dedekind-of-Dedekind, and `Σ_{Q over P} e(Q|P) · f(Q|P) = d`).
126	  This is `~50-100 LOC` glue around the existing Mathlib lemma.
127	
128	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (with `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` as
129	  the body-engine for iter-178+).
130	
131	### Decision 3: multiplicativity of degree
132	
133	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic`
134	  (`sum_ramification_inertia`, `ramificationIdx_mul_inertiaDeg_of_isLocalRing`)
135	  is the algebraic statement. There is no scheme-level wrapper.
136	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (consume the ring-level statement inside
137	  the body).
138	
139	## Recommendation
140	
141	Both the **signature** and the eventual **body** for Pin 2 are
142	project-bespoke — Mathlib has the algebraic atom
143	(`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` in
144	`Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Basic`) but no scheme-level
145	divisor / morphism-degree wrapper to align with. The iter-178+ planner
146	should:
147	
148	1. Keep the current signature of `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` (it
149	   is the right substantive content given the project-bespoke
150	   `Scheme.WeilDivisor`).
151	2. Plan to introduce a private `Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback` (or a
152	   one-shot pole-divisor construction `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` for
153	   `φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹`) inside `RationalCurveIso.lean` or
154	   `WeilDivisor.lean`.
155	3. Reduce the multiplicativity-of-degree identity through
156	   `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` on a chosen affine cover (the
157	   only point of contact with Mathlib). Budget: ~80-150 LOC for the
158	   pullback + the per-chart Dedekind-domain bridge.
159	
160	No Mathlib **upstream PR** is required — this is genuine new
161	infrastructure that Mathlib lacks, not a re-implementation. If the
162	project ever exports a `Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback`-style API, it
163	*would* be worth a future Mathlib PR (Stacks tags 02RW, 02RX, 0AX5
164	cover this material), but that is out of scope for the iter-181/182
165	plan.
166	
167	## Possible Mathlib gap-fill (informational, not blocking)
168	
169	For the eventual upstream PR (post-project), the following would close
170	the gap in Mathlib:
171	
172	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.degree : (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIntegral X]
173	  [IsIntegral Y] [IsFinite f] [LocallyOfFiniteType f] : ℕ`
174	  defined as `Module.finrank Y.functionField X.functionField`.
175	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor` (currently project-bespoke).
176	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.pullback (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsFinite f] :
177	  Y.WeilDivisor →+ X.WeilDivisor`.
178	- `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_pullback` (Hartshorne II.6.9):
179	  `(f^*D).degree = f.degree * D.degree`.
180	
181	The project must not block on any of these — the in-tree route is the
182	correct call for iter-181/182.

10	# Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap
159	
160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
192	
193	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
194	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
195	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
196	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
197	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
198	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
199	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
200	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
201	
202	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
205	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
206	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
207	    (f : X.functionField) :
208	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
209	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
210	  sorry

216	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
217	
218	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
219	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
220	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
221	
222	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
223	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
224	
225	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
226	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
227	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
228	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
229	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
230	
231	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
232	
233	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
234	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
235	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
236	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
237	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
238	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
239	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
240	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
241	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
242	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
243	hypothesised regime. -/
244	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
245	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
246	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
247	
248	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
249	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
250	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
251	multiplicity one. -/
252	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
253	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
254	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
255	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
256	
257	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
258	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
259	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
260	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
261	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
262	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
263	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
264	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
265	
266	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
267	
268	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
269	
270	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
271	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
272	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
273	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
274	
275	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
276	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
277	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
278	
279	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
280	
281	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
282	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
283	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
284	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
285	
286	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
287	
288	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
289	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
290	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
291	
292	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
293	
294	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
295	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
296	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
297	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
298	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
299	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
300	
301	@[simp]
302	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
303	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
304	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
305	
306	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
307	
308	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
309	
310	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
311	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
312	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
313	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
314	
315	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
316	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
319	following definition, p. 131).
320	
321	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
322	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
323	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
324	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
325	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
326	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
327	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
328	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
329	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
330	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
331	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
332	
333	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
334	
335	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
336	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
337	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
340	
341	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
342	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
343	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
344	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
345	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
346	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
347	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
348	  toFun u :=
349	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
350	  map_one' := by
351	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
352	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
353	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
354	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
355	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
356	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
357	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
358	    congr 1
359	    apply Finsupp.ext
360	    intro Y
361	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
362	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
363	    rw [Units.val_one]
364	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
365	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
366	  map_mul' u v := by
367	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
368	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
369	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
370	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
371	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
372	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
373	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
374	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
375	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
376	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
377	    congr 1
378	    apply Finsupp.ext
379	    intro Y
380	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
381	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
382	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
383	    rw [Units.val_mul]
384	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
385	    rw [map_mul]
386	    exact WithZero.log_mul
387	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
388	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
389	
390	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
391	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
392	
393	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
394	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
395	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
396	
397	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
398	
399	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
400	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
401	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
402	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
403	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
404	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
405	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
406	iters of `RR.1`. -/
407	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
408	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
409	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
410	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
411	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
412	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
413	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
414	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
415	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
416	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
417	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
418	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
419	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
420	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
421	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
422	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
423	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
424	      apply Finsupp.ext
425	      intro Y
426	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
427	      exact hconst Y
428	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
429	    unfold degree
430	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
431	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
432	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
433	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
434	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
435	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
436	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
437	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
438	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
439	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
440	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
441	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
442	    sorry
443	
444	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
445	
446	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
447	
448	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
449	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
450	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
451	
452	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
453	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
454	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
455	**divisor class group** of `X`.
456	
457	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
458	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
459	
460	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
461	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
462	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
463	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
464	
465	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
466	
467	end AlgebraicGeometry
